164.Plants research (植物研究)3 格#080166
Plants serve as the conduit of energy into the biosphere, provide food and materials used by humans, and they 1shape our environment. According to Ehrhardt and Frommer, the three major challenges facing humanity in our time are food, energy, and environmental 2degradation . All three are plant related. All of our food is produced by plants, either directly or indirectly via animals that eat them. Plants are a 3source of energy production. And they are intimately involved in climate change and a major factor in a variety of environmental concerns, including agricultural expansion and its impact on habitat destruction and waterway pollution. What's more, none of these issues is independent of each other. Climate change places additional stresses on the food supply and on various habitats. So, plant research is instrumental in addressing all of these problems and moving into the future. For plant research to move significantly forward, Ehrhardt and Former say technological development is critical, both to test existing hypotheses and to gain new information and generate fresh hypotheses. If we are to make headway in understanding how these essential organisms function and build the foundation for a sustainable future, then we need to apply the most advanced technologies available to the study of plant life, they say.
空格 1shape grow / shape / model / content
搭配「shape our environment」固定搭配，指形塑環境
語意與 provide food and materials 並列，指植物形塑我們的環境
✗ grow/model/content 皆不能搭配 our environment 表「形塑」
空格 2degradation discrimination / demotion / degradation / production
搭配「environmental degradation」固定搭配，指環境惡化
語意與 food, energy 並列為三大挑戰，後文 habitat destruction, pollution 呼應惡化
✗ discrimination 歧視、demotion 降職、production 生產，皆不搭 environmental 表惡化
空格 3source resource / source / root / outcome
搭配「a source of energy production」固定講法，指能源生產的來源
單複數前有 a，需用單數可數 source
✗ resource 雖近義但 a source of 更自然、root 偏根源、outcome 為結果語意相反
165.Dark Energy (暗能量)4 格#080167
The rest of the universe appears to be made of a mysterious, invisible 1substance called dark matter The rest of the universe appears to be made of a mysterious, invisible substance called dark matter 25% and a force that repels gravity known as dark energy 70%. Scientists have not yet 2observed dark matter directly. It doesn't interact with baryonic matter; it's completely invisible to light and other matter directly. It doesn't interact with baryonic matter; it's completely invisible to light and other forms of electromagnetic radiation, making dark matter 3impossible to detect with current instruments. But scientists are confident it 4exists because of the gravitational effects, it appears to have on galaxies observed observe been_observed being_observed unaccessible impossible ridiculous unbelievable exists lives develops forms
空格 1substance environment / substance / theory / atmosphere
搭配「invisible substance」搭配，指看不見的物質
語意a mysterious, invisible ___ called dark matter，指稱暗物質為一種物質
✗ environment 環境、theory 理論、atmosphere 大氣，皆不搭 invisible...called dark matter
空格 2observed observed / observe
時態「have not yet ___ dark matter」現在完成式需過去分詞 observed
搭配have not yet + p.p. 表「尚未…」
✗ observe 為原形，無法構成 have not yet + 過去分詞
空格 3impossible unaccessible / impossible / ridiculous / unbelievable
搭配「impossible to detect」固定搭配，指無法偵測
語意completely invisible...making dark matter ___ to detect，指完全看不見故無法偵測
✗ unaccessible 非標準字（應為 inaccessible）、ridiculous 荒謬、unbelievable 難以置信，皆語意不合
空格 4exists exists / lives / develops / forms
搭配「confident it exists」固定講法，指確信其存在
語意scientists are confident it ___ because of gravitational effects，指確信暗物質存在
✗ lives 用於生物、develops 發展、forms 形成，皆不搭 confident it ___ 表「存在」
166.Retirement (退休)5 格#080168
For a start, we need to change our 1concept of 'retirement', and we need to change mind-sets arising from earlier government policy which, in the face of high unemployment levels, encouraged mature from earlier government policy which, in the face of high unemployment levels, encouraged mature workers to take early retirement. Today, government encourages them to 2delay their retirement. We now need to think of retirement as a phased process, where mature age workers 3gradually reduce their now need to think of retirement as a phased process, where mature age workers gradually reduce their hours, and where they have considerable flexibility in how they combine their work and non work time. We also need to recognise the broader change that is occurring in how people work, learn, and live. We also need to recognise the broader change that is occurring in how people work, learn, and live. Increasingly we are moving away from a linear relationship between education, training, work, and retirement, as people move in and out of jobs, careers, caregiving, study, and leisure. Employers of retirement, as people move in and out of jobs, careers, caregiving, study, and leisure. Employers of choice remove the 4barriers between the different segments of people's lives, by creating flexible conditions of work and a range of leave entitlements. They take an individualised approach to conditions of work and a range of leave entitlements. They take an individualised approach to workforce planning and development so that the needs of employers and employees can be met 5simultaneously . This approach supports the different transitions that occur across the life course-for simultaneously . This approach supports the different transitions that occur across the life course-for example, school to work, becoming a parent, becoming responsible for the care of older relatives, and moving from work to retirement. moving from work to retirement. unexpectedly hastily gradually surprisingly bonds correlations borders barriers separately simultaneously respectively individually
空格 1concept intention / concept / expectation / conceptual
搭配「change our concept of」固定搭配，指改變對某事的概念
單複數our ___ of 'retirement' 需單數可數名詞 concept
✗ intention 意圖、expectation 期待、conceptual 為形容詞詞性錯誤，皆不搭 change our ___ of
空格 2delay delay / implement / complement / supplement
語意與前句 early retirement 對比，政府現在鼓勵延後退休
搭配「delay their retirement」固定搭配，指延後退休
✗ implement 執行、complement/supplement 補充，皆與 retirement 無「延後」語意
空格 3gradually unexpectedly / hastily / gradually / surprisingly
詞性workers ___ reduce their hours 需副詞修飾動詞 reduce
語意呼應 phased process（分階段過程），指逐漸減少工時
✗ unexpectedly/surprisingly 意外地、hastily 倉促地，皆與分階段逐步相反
空格 4barriers bonds / correlations / borders / barriers
搭配「remove the barriers between」固定搭配，指移除…之間的障礙
語意remove the ___ between the different segments of people's lives，指消除生活區隔的障礙
✗ bonds 連結、correlations 相關、borders 偏疆界，皆不如 barriers 搭 remove...between
空格 5simultaneously separately / simultaneously / respectively / individually
語意needs of employers and employees can be met ___，指雙方需求同時被滿足
詞性句末副詞修飾 be met，simultaneously 表同時
✗ separately/individually 分別地與雙方一起滿足矛盾、respectively 各自地語意不合

168.Linda Finch (琳達·芬奇)5 格#080170
Sixty years after Amelia Earhart vanished mysteriously in the Pacific during her attempt to become the first person to circumnavigate the world along the equator, Linda Finch, a San Antonio businesswoman, accomplished pilot, and aviation historian, recreated and completed her idol's last flight as a 1tribute to the aviation pioneer's spirit and vision. On March 17, 1997, Ms. Finch and a flight as a tribute to the aviation pioneer's spirit and vision. On March 17, 1997, Ms. Finch and a navigator took off from Oakland International Airport, California, in a restored Lockheed Electra 10E, the same make and model 2aircraft that Earhart used on her last journey. The mission to fulfill Amelia the same make and model aircraft that Earhart used on her last journey. The mission to fulfill Amelia Earhart's dream was called “World Flight 1997.” Although Ms. Finch was not the first to 3attempt Earhart's around-the-world journey, she was the first to do it in a historic airplane. Linda Finch closely Earhart's around-the-world journey, she was the first to do it in a historic airplane. Linda Finch closely followed the same route that Earhart flew, stopping in 18 countries before finishing the trip two and a half months later when she landed back at the Oakland Airport on May 28. Over a million school half months later when she landed back at the Oakland Airport on May 28. Over a million school children and others were able to follow the flight 4daily through an interactive website as part of a free multimedia 5educational program called “You Can Soar,” provided by the project's sponsor. multimedia educational program called “You Can Soar,” provided by the project's sponsor. journey flight aircraft trip attempt contempt tempt contemn daily nightly yearly hourly

✗ attribution 歸因、contribution 貢獻、attribute 屬性，皆無 as a ___ to 致敬之意
空格 2aircraft journey / flight / aircraft / trip
搭配「the same make and model aircraft」固定講法，指同款飛機

✗ journey/flight/trip 為旅程，與 make and model（廠牌型號）搭配不合，唯飛機有型號

搭配「attempt + 名詞」指嘗試做某事，此處 attempt Earhart's journey

✗ contempt 蔑視、tempt 引誘、contemn 鄙視，皆與嘗試旅程語意不合

詞性副詞修飾 follow，daily 表每日
✗ nightly 每夜、yearly 每年、hourly 每小時，皆與每日追蹤頻率不合

169.Olympic medallists (奧運會獎牌獲得者)6 格#080171
In an often-cited study about counterfactuals, Medvec, Madey, and Gilovich (1995) found that bronze medalists appeared happier than silver medalists in television coverage of the 1992 Summer Olympics. medalists appeared happier than silver medalists in television coverage of the 1992 Summer Olympics. Medvec et al. 1argued that bronze medalists compared themselves to 4th place finishers, 2whereas silver medalists compared themselves to gold medalists. These counterfactuals were the most 3salient medalists compared themselves to gold medalists. These counterfactuals were the most salient because they were either qualitatively different (gold vs. silver) or categorically different (medal vs. no medal) from what 4actually occurred. Drawing on archival data and experimental studies, we show that medal) from what actually occurred. Drawing on archival data and experimental studies, we show that Olympic athletes (among others) are more likely to make counterfactual comparisons based on their 5prior expectations, consistent with decision affect theory. Silver medalists are more likely to be prior expectations, consistent with decision affect theory. Silver medalists are more likely to be disappointed because their personal expectations are higher than 6those of bronze medalists.
空格 1argued rejected / argued / assumed / presumed
時態「Medvec et al. ___ that」敘述研究主張，過去式 argued
語意後接 that 子句提出論點，指研究者主張
✗ rejected 駁回語意相反、assumed/presumed 偏假定，皆不如 argued 表提出論點
空格 2whereas therefore / whereas / despite / although
搭配whereas 引導對比子句，意為「然而、相對地」
語意銅牌比 4th place，___ 銀牌比金牌，前後形成對比
✗ therefore 因果、despite 後接名詞、although 讓步，皆不如 whereas 表並列對比
空格 3salient salient / resilient / relevant / inconspicuous
詞性the most ___ 需形容詞，salient 指顯著的
語意「the most salient counterfactuals」指最突出顯著的反事實想法
✗ resilient 有韌性、relevant 相關、inconspicuous 不顯眼（語意相反），皆不合
空格 4actually consequently / possibly / actually / probably
搭配「what actually occurred」固定講法，指實際發生的事
詞性副詞修飾 occurred，actually 表實際上
✗ consequently 因此、possibly/probably 表可能性，皆不搭 what ___ occurred 表「實際」
空格 5prior post / prior / outward / future
搭配「prior expectations」固定搭配，指先前的預期
語意comparisons based on their ___ expectations，呼應事前預期
✗ post 事後、outward 向外、future 未來，皆與事前預期語意不合
空格 6those that / this / these / those
單複數替代複數 expectations，需用 those（those of bronze medalists）
語意higher than ___ of bronze medalists，比較兩組人的預期，用 those 指代
✗ that 用於單數、this/these 為指示「這」而非比較中替代 the ones，皆不合

Last year remittances sent home by migrants were expected to 3exceed $232 billion according to the World Bank which 4tracks these figures migrants were expected to exceed $232 billion according to the World Bank which tracks these figures vital through the flow of remittances is to alleviate the plight of the migrant’s family, it can’t on its own lift entire nation out of poverty. Those who study the 5impacts of remittances argue that money own lift entire nation out of poverty. ✗ supplement 補充、pass 通過皆不搭金額；surpass 雖近義但 exceed 為金額慣用

✗ outcomes 結果、origins 起源、enforcements 執行，皆不如 impacts 搭 study...of 表影響
171.Spanish language (西班牙語)5 格#080173
If after years of Spanish classes, some people still find it impossible to understand some native speakers, they should not worry. This does not 1necessarily mean the lessons were wasted. Millions of Spanish speakers use neither standard Latin American Spanish nor Castilian, which predominate in US schools. The confusion is partly political-the Spanish-speaking world is very diverse. Spanish is the language of 19 separate countries and Puerto Rico. This means that there is no one standard dialect. The most common Spanish dialect taught in the US is standard Latin American. It is sometimes called "Highland" Spanish since it is generally spoken in the 2mountainous areas of Latin America. While each country retains its own 3accents and has some unique vocabulary, residents of countries such as Mexico, Colombia, Peru, and Bolivia generally speak Latin American Spanish, especially in urban centers. This dialect is noted for its 4pronunciation of each letter and its strong "r" sounds. This Spanish was spoken in Spain in the sixteenth and seventeenth centuries and was brought to the Americas by the early colonists. However, the Spanish of Madrid and of northern Spain, called Castilian, developed 5characteristics that never reached the New World. These include the pronunciation of "ci" and "ce" as "th." In Madrid, "gracias" (thank you) becomes "gratheas" (as opposed to "gras-see-as" in Latin America). Another difference is the use of the word "vosotros" (you all, or you guys) as the informal form of "ustedes" in Spain. Castilian sounds to Latin Americans much like British English sounds to US announcement elucidation pronunciation presentation characteristics idiocrasy normality peculiarity
空格 1necessarily necessarily / merely / especially / particularly
搭配「not necessarily」固定搭配，指「未必、不一定」
語意does not ___ mean the lessons were wasted，指未必表示課程白費
✗ merely 僅僅、especially/particularly 特別地，皆不搭 not ___ mean 表「未必」
空格 2mountainous aqueous / hidden / mountainous / coastal
語意呼應 called Highland Spanish，指通行於拉美山區
搭配「mountainous areas」固定搭配，指山區
✗ aqueous 水的、hidden 隱藏、coastal 沿海，皆與 Highland（高地）不符
空格 3accents thoughts / voices / accents / customs
搭配「retains its own accents」固定搭配，指各國保有自己的口音
語意與 unique vocabulary 並列，指各國的口音與詞彙
✗ thoughts 想法、voices 嗓音、customs 習俗，皆不如 accents 搭語言差異
空格 4pronunciation announcement / elucidation / pronunciation / presentation
搭配「pronunciation of each letter」固定講法，指每個字母的發音
語意noted for its ___ of each letter and strong r sounds，描述發音特徵
✗ announcement 公告、elucidation 闡明、presentation 呈現，皆與 each letter/r sounds 不合
空格 5characteristics characteristics / idiocrasy / normality / peculiarity
搭配「developed characteristics」固定搭配，指發展出某些特徵
語意Castilian developed ___ that never reached the New World，指發展出特性
✗ idiocrasy 為非標準字、normality 常態、peculiarity 偏怪癖，皆不如 characteristics 自然
172.Anesthesia (麻醉)3 格#080174
Before effective anaesthetics, surgery was very crude and very painful. Before 1800, alcohol and opium had 1little success in easing pain during operations. Laughing gas was used in 1844 in dentistry in the USA, but failed to ease all pain and patients 2remained conscious. Ether (used from 1846) made patients totally unconscious and lasted a long time. However, it could make patients cough during operations and sick afterwards. It was highly flammable and was 3transported in heavy glass bottles. Chloroform (used from 1847) was very effective with few side effects. However, it was difficult to get the dose right and could kill some people because of the effect on their heart. An inhaler helped to regulate the because_of other_than rather_than apart_from 4 because_of other_than rather_than apart_from
空格 1little little / much / less / no

✗ much 表很多語意相反、less 為比較級無比較對象、no 過於絕對語氣突兀
空格 2remained migrated / remained / retained / maintained
搭配「remained conscious」固定搭配，指仍保持清醒
語意failed to ease all pain and patients ___ conscious，指病人仍清醒
✗ migrated 遷移、retained/maintained 為及物需受詞，皆不搭 ___ conscious 此繫動詞用法
空格 3transported transported / transmitted / transparent / transformed
搭配「transported in bottles」固定搭配，指以瓶子運送
語意was highly flammable and was ___ in heavy glass bottles，指被運送
✗ transmitted 傳輸訊號、transparent 透明（形容詞）、transformed 轉變，皆與運送不合

174.Tomkins (湯姆金斯)4 格#080176
Much of our understanding of mind-reading comes from two remarkable scientists, a teacher and his pupil: Silvan Tomkins and Paul Ekman. Tomkins was the teacher. He was born in Philadelphia, at the pupil: Silvan Tomkins and Paul Ekman. Tomkins was the teacher. He was born in Philadelphia, at the turn of the last 1century , the son of a dentist from Russia. He was short, and thick around the middle, with a wild mane of white hair and huge black plastic-rimmed glasses. He taught psychology at with a wild mane of white hair and huge black plastic-rimmed glasses. He taught psychology at Princeton and Rutgers, and was the author of “Affect, Imagery, Consciousness, “a four-volume work so 2dense that its readers were evenly divided between those who understood it and thought it was so dense that its readers were evenly divided between those who understood it and thought it was brilliant and those who did not understand it and thought it was brilliant. He was a 3legendary talker. At the end of a cocktail party, a crowd of people would sit, rapt, at Tomkins’s feet, and someone would the end of a cocktail party, a crowd of people would sit, rapt, at Tomkins’s feet, and someone would say, “One more question! “ and they would all sit there for another hour and a half, as Tomkins held forth on, say, comic books, a television sitcom, the biology of emotion, his problem with Kant, and his forth on, say, comic books, a television sitcom, the biology of emotion, his problem with Kant, and his enthusiasm for the latest fad diets, all enfolded into one 4extended riff.
空格 1century decade / millennium / epoch / century
搭配「the turn of the last century」固定講法，指上世紀之交
語意born at the turn of the last ___，指世紀交替之際出生
✗ decade 十年、millennium 千年、epoch 紀元，皆不搭 the turn of the last ___ 此慣用語
空格 2dense laborious / dense / readily / deliberate
搭配「so dense that」搭配，形容文字艱澀濃密
語意a four-volume work so ___ that readers were divided，指著作艱深難懂
✗ laborious 費力、readily 為副詞詞性錯誤、deliberate 刻意，皆不如 dense 形容艱深
空格 3legendary legendary / irascible / uninspiring / garrulous
詞性a ___ talker 需形容詞修飾 talker
語意「a legendary talker」指傳奇般的健談者，呼應後文眾人圍坐聆聽
✗ irascible 易怒、uninspiring 乏味（語意相反）、garrulous 雖指多話但帶貶意，不如 legendary 正面突出
空格 4extended amplified / illusive / extended / unhappy
搭配「one extended riff」固定搭配，指一段延展的即興發揮
語意all enfolded into one ___ riff，指各話題交織成冗長連貫的長篇
✗ amplified 放大、illusive 虛幻、unhappy 不快，皆不如 extended 形容話題延展
175.Three degrees (叁度)4 格#080177
Three degrees does not sound like much but it 1represents a rise in temperature compatible with the Three degrees does not sound like much but it represents a rise in temperature compatible with the global heating that occurred between the last ice age, some 15,000 years ago, and the warmth of the eighteenth century. When Earth was cold, giant glaciers sometimes extended from the polar-regions as eighteenth century. When Earth was cold, giant glaciers sometimes extended from the polar-regions as far south as St Louis in the US and the Alps in Europe. Later this century when it is three degree, hotter glaciers everywhere will be melting in a climate of often 2unbearable heat and drought punctuated with glaciers everywhere will be melting in a climate of often unbearable heat and drought punctuated with storms and floods. The 3consequences for humanity could be truly horrific, if we fail to act swiftly, the full impact of global heating could cull us along with vast populations of the plant and animals with full impact of global heating could cull us along with vast populations of the plant and animals with whom we share Earth. In a worst case scenario, there might-in the 22nd century-be only a remnant of humanity eking out a 4diminished existence in the polar-regions and the few remaining oases left on a humanity eking out a diminished existence in the polar-regions and the few remaining oases left on a hot and arid Earth.
空格 1represents displays / represents / declares / discloses
搭配「represents a rise」固定搭配，指代表一種上升
語意it ___ a rise in temperature compatible with...，指三度代表某種升溫
✗ displays 展示、declares 宣布、discloses 揭露，皆不搭 a rise 表「代表」
空格 2unbearable insurable / endurable / unbearable / admissible
搭配「unbearable heat」固定搭配，指難以忍受的高溫
語意a climate of often ___ heat and drought，指酷熱難耐
✗ insurable 可保的、endurable 可忍受（語意相反）、admissible 可接受，皆不合酷熱情境
空格 3consequences preclusions / achievements / consequences / obstructions
搭配「the consequences for humanity」固定搭配，指對人類的後果
語意The ___ for humanity could be truly horrific，指可怕的後果
✗ preclusions 排除、achievements 成就（語意相反）、obstructions 阻礙，皆不如 consequences 表後果
空格 4diminished magnified / ampliefied / depreciated / diminished
搭配「diminished existence」固定搭配，指衰減的存在／生活
語意remnant of humanity eking out a ___ existence，指殘存人類過著萎縮的生活
✗ magnified/ampliefied 放大（且後者拼錯）、depreciated 貶值，皆與 remnant/eking out 不合
176.Retirement savings (退休儲蓄)5 格#080178
Men and women are making different choices about their retirement savings, which could lead to very different investment outcomes, according to Dr Claire Matthews, Director of Financial Planning at Massey University's Centre for Banking Studies. Speaking at the 2012 New Zealand Finance Colloquium, held at Massey University's Albany campus last week, Dr Matthews said demographic characteristics had a substantial impact on the choices people made about KiwiSaver funds and retirement savings more generally. 1When it comes to fund selection, she found there were significant differences based on gender. Men are more likely to invest in aggressive and 2growth funds, while women are more likely to choose conservative funds. "Males are risk takers, 3whether it's in their choice of car or their investment fund," she says. "But when it comes to long-term savings, risk taking can actually be an advantage." Dr Matthews also found that men are more likely than women to have prior savings when joining KiwiSaver. Just over half of male respondents said they had savings already, while only 38% of women did. "These figures reflect and confirm, quite disappointingly, the difference between males and females and the level of interest they take in financial planning," Dr Matthews says. "It's important for all New Zealanders to be better educated about their personal finances, but this is particularly so for women." Other demographic factors, including age, ethnicity, education, and income, can also influence the choices 4being made about retirement savings. Dr Matthews found that those with bachelor and higher degrees, and those in households with a pre-tax income of $100,000 or more, were more likely to choose aggressive and growth funds. 5On the other hand , both the youngest and oldest age groups were more likely to be invested in conservative funds. While this might be appropriate for the life-cycle stage of older investors, it might not be so appropriate for younger, 1 When_it_comes_to Noting_that Despite Given_that being of_being are were Despite_that As_a_result On_the_other_hand Fairly_speaking 5 Despite_that As_a_result On_the_other_hand Fairly_speaking
空格 1When it comes to When it comes to / Noting that / Despite / Given that
搭配When it comes to「談到…時」為固定片語，後接名詞 fund selection
✗ Noting that、Given that 後須接子句而非僅名詞片語；Despite 語意不符。
空格 2growth growth / balanced / risky / passive
搭配growth funds「成長型基金」為投資固定術語，與 aggressive 並列，後文反覆出現 aggressive and growth funds
✗ balanced、risky、passive 皆非與 aggressive 並列且後文重複使用的基金類別用詞。
空格 3whether both / whether / what if / although
搭配whether it's... or...「無論是…或…」，whether 搭配後文 or their investment fund
✗ both 須搭 and 非 or；what if、although 皆不構成 whether...or 的選擇結構。
空格 4being being / of being / are / were
詞性介系詞 about 後接動名詞 being「對…有興趣的程度」，the level of interest they take in...being
時態需動名詞形式 being，非限定動詞
✗ are、were 為限定動詞無法接於介系詞後；of being 多出冗贅介系詞。
空格 5On the other hand Despite that / As a result / On the other hand / Fairly speaking
語意語意：前述高學歷高收入者選積極型基金，「另一方面」最年輕與最年長者選保守型，對比
✗ Despite that、As a result、Fairly speaking 皆不符兩群投資人選擇對比的銜接關係。
177.David Lynch (大衛·林奇)4 格#080179
David Lynch is professor and head of education at Charles Darwin University. 1Prior to this he was sub dean in the Faculty of Education and Creative Arts at Central Queensland University and foundation head of the University’s Noosa 2campus . David’s career in education began as a primary school teacher in Queensland in the early 1980’s and 3progressed to four principal positions before 4entering higher education. David’s research interests predominate in teacher education with particular interest in building teacher capability to meet a changed world. involving entering locating achieving
空格 1Prior Prior / Early / Before / Post
搭配固定搭配 Prior to this（在此之前），後面接 to this 只有 Prior 對
語意語意上承接前句、講「之前」的經歷
✗ Before 後不接 to；Early/Post 與 to this 不搭也語意不合
空格 2campus campus / college / district / site
搭配Noosa campus（諾薩校區）為固定用法，搭配大學分部
語意foundation head of the University's ___ 指大學的「校區」
✗ college/district/site 與 University's 校區語意不貼切
空格 3progressed regressed / progressed / processed / retarded
語意career began...and ___ to higher education，職涯由教師「進展」到高教，正向
搭配progress to 為固定搭配（進展到下一階段）
✗ regressed/retarded 為退步、負向；processed 不搭 to 也語意不合
空格 4entering involving / entering / locating / achieving
搭配before entering higher education（進入高教之前）固定搭配 enter + 領域
語意與前文 progressed...before ___ 對應，指「進入」高教
✗ involving/locating/achieving 與 higher education 語意不合
178.EE & AVG V1 (EE&平均值V1)5 格#080180
Currently, there is concern about the increasing amount of time children spend 1engaged in sedentary Currently, there is concern about the increasing amount of time children spend engaged in sedentary activities, the number of children who fail to achieve 2minimum daily physical activity guidelines (i.e. 60 min of moderate-to-vigorous intensity activities every day), and the apparent increase in obesity min of moderate-to-vigorous intensity activities every day), and the apparent increase in obesity prevalence as a result of such sedentary 3behavior . Screen-based activities, including television viewing and playing computer games, are among the most frequently observed sedentary activities that and playing computer games, are among the most frequently observed sedentary activities that children participate, with children spending 2.5-4 h per day participating in such activities. The introduction of “active video games” (AVGs) into the gaming market presents an opportunity to introduction of “active video games” (AVGs) into the gaming market presents an opportunity to convert traditional, sedentary screen-time in to active screen-time and thus increase total daily energy expenditure (EE). Modern AVGs utilize cameras and motion sensors to allow the gamer to physically expenditure (EE). Modern AVGs utilize cameras and motion sensors to allow the gamer to physically perform a variety of actions, 4dependent on the console, such as swinging a tennis racquet or running. The most demanding AVGs provided similar responses to walking and, based on international The most demanding AVGs provided similar responses to walking and, based on international standards, should be classified as low-intensity activities. Whilst AVGs may provide children with a better alternative to sedentary gaming, they are not a sufficient 5replacement for normal physical better alternative to sedentary gaming, they are not a sufficient replacement for normal physical activity, e.g. sports and outdoor play.
空格 1engaged engaged / carried / promoted / keen
搭配固定搭配 engaged in（從事、投入某活動）
語意spend...time ___ in sedentary activities 指投入久坐活動
✗ carried/promoted/keen 與 in activities 搭配不成立
空格 2minimum minimum / maximum / average / routine
語意fail to achieve ___ daily guidelines，指未達「最低」每日標準
搭配minimum guidelines（最低標準）符合 60 min 的下限語境
✗ maximum 與「未達標」矛盾；average/routine 語意不合
空格 3behavior action / behavior / gesture / motion
搭配固定搭配 sedentary behavior（久坐行為）為文中反覆出現的術語
語意as a result of such sedentary ___ 指肥胖源於久坐「行為」
✗ action/gesture/motion 偏單一動作，不指長期行為模式
空格 4dependent dependent / rely / upon / subject
詞性需形容詞接 on，dependent on（取決於）；rely/upon/subject 在此填不成 dependent on 結構
搭配dependent on the console（依主機而定）固定搭配
✗ rely 為動詞；upon 是介系詞；subject 須 subject to 才成立
空格 5replacement placement / replacement / boost / correspondent
搭配固定搭配 a sufficient replacement for（足以取代…）
語意not a sufficient ___ for normal physical activity 指無法「取代」正常運動
✗ placement/boost/correspondent 與 for normal activity 語意不合
179.EE & AVG V2 (EE&平均值V2)5 格#080181
Here has been increased research interest in the use of active video games (in which players physically Here has been increased research interest in the use of active video games (in which players physically interact with images onscreen) as a means to 1promote physical activity in children. The aim of this review was to assess active video games as a means of increasing energy expenditure and physical review was to assess active video games as a means of increasing energy expenditure and physical activity behavior in children. Studies were obtained from computerized searches of multiple electronic bibliographic databases. The last search was conducted in December 2008. Eleven studies focused on bibliographic databases. The last search was conducted in December 2008. Eleven studies focused on the quantification of the energy cost associated with playing active video games, and eight studies focused on the utility of active video games as an 2intervention to increase physical activity in children. focused on the utility of active video games as an intervention to increase physical activity in children. Compared with traditional non-active video games, active video games 3elicited greater energy expenditure, which was similar in intensity to mild to moderate intensity physical activity. The expenditure, which was similar in intensity to mild to moderate intensity physical activity. The intervention studies indicate that active video games may have the potential to increase free-living physical activity and improve body composition in children; however, methodological limitations physical activity and improve body composition in children; however, methodological limitations prevent 4definitive conclusions. Future research should focus on larger, methodologically sound intervention trials to provide definitive answers as to whether this technology is effective in 5promoting intervention trials to provide definitive answers as to whether this technology is effective in promoting long-term physical activity in children.
空格 1promote promote / obstruct / impede / prejudice
語意as a means to ___ physical activity，正向語境指「促進」身體活動
搭配promote physical activity 固定搭配
✗ obstruct/impede/prejudice 皆為阻礙、負向，與全文推廣立場相反
空格 2intervention promotion / intervention / invasion / infringement
搭配固定搭配 as an intervention to increase（作為提升…的介入手段）
語意學術語境指研究「介入」措施
✗ promotion 偏宣傳；invasion/infringement 為侵犯，語意不合
空格 3elicited decreased / summoned / elicited / spreaded
時態主詞 active video games + 過去式 ___ greater energy expenditure，需及物動詞過去式
搭配elicit a response/expenditure（引發…）固定搭配
✗ decreased 與 greater 矛盾；summoned 召喚語意不合；spreaded 非正確拼法
空格 4definitive definitive / tentative / provisional / optimal
語意limitations prevent ___ conclusions，限制使其無法下「確定」結論
搭配definitive conclusions/answers（確定結論）固定搭配，與後文 definitive answers 呼應
✗ tentative/provisional 為暫時、初步，與 prevent 語意矛盾；optimal 不搭
空格 5promoting obstructing / indicating / engaging / promoting
時態effective in ___ 需動名詞，effective in promoting（有效促進）
語意全文主旨在「促進」長期身體活動
✗ obstructing 阻礙矛盾；indicating/engaging 與 long-term physical activity 語意不合
180.The growth of the internet (互聯網的發展)3 格#080182
The exponential growth of the internet was 1heralded , in the 1990s, as revolutionizing the production The exponential growth of the internet was heralded , in the 1990s, as revolutionizing the production and dissemination of information. Some people saw the internet as a means of democratizing access to knowledge. For people 2concerned with African development, it seemed to offer the possibility of 3leapfrogging over the technology gap that separates Africa from advanced industrialized countries. leapfrogging over the technology gap that separates Africa from advanced industrialized countries. compared worried concerned correlated fetching leapfrogging turning jogging
空格 1heralded created / innovated / shouted / heralded
時態was ___ 被動式需過去分詞；was heralded as（被譽為、被預示為）
搭配heralded as revolutionizing 固定搭配，指被大肆宣告
✗ created/innovated 與 as revolutionizing 語意不合；shouted 不搭被動 as 結構
空格 2concerned compared / worried / concerned / correlated
搭配固定搭配 people concerned with（關心…的人），後接 with
語意For people ___ with African development 指關注非洲發展者
✗ worried with 不成立（worried about）；compared/correlated with 語意不合
空格 3leapfrogging fetching / leapfrogging / turning / jogging
搭配leapfrog over the gap（跳躍式跨越差距）固定搭配，後接 over
語意___ over the technology gap 指越過技術鴻溝
✗ fetching/turning/jogging 與 over the technology gap 語意不合
181.Conflict (沖突)5 格#080183
Most of us are 1scared of open conflict and avoid it if we can. And there is a 2risk to expressing and working through conflict. If the working involves harsh words and name-calling people fell deeply hurt and relationships can be 3damaged . Some group members may be afraid that if they really 4express their anger, they may go out of control and become violent, or they may do this. These fears can be very 5real and based on experience. encouraged scared horrible panic
空格 1scared encouraged / scared / horrible / panic
詞性are ___ of 需形容詞作主詞補語；be scared of（害怕）
搭配scared of 固定搭配，後接 of conflict
✗ encouraged of 不成立；horrible 形容事物非感受；panic 為名詞/動詞
空格 2risk risk / challenge / obstacle / hazard
搭配固定搭配 a risk to expressing（…的風險），後接 to
語意後文以 hurt、damaged 說明這是「風險」
✗ challenge/obstacle/hazard 與 to expressing 搭配不順、語意較弱
空格 3damaged impaired / destroyed / weakened / damaged
時態relationships can be ___ 被動需過去分詞
搭配relationships can be damaged（關係受損）固定搭配
✗ impaired/weakened 程度較輕；destroyed 過重，relationships 慣用 damaged
空格 4express express / communicate / suppress / conceal
語意if they really ___ their anger 指「表達」憤怒，與後文 go out of control 因果連貫
搭配express anger（表達憤怒）固定搭配
✗ suppress/conceal 為壓抑、隱藏，與失控的因果矛盾；communicate 不搭 anger
空格 5real substitute / real / tangible / artificial
搭配固定搭配 very real（非常真實），and based on experience 呼應
語意These fears can be very ___ 指恐懼「真實」存在
✗ substitute/artificial 與「真實」相反；tangible 多指可觸摸的具體物
182.Seatbelt (安全帶)4 格#080184
I am a cyclist and a motorist. I fasten my seatbelt when I drive and wear a helmet on my bike to reduce the risk of injury. I am convinced that these are prudent safety measures. I have persuaded many friends to wear helmets on the grounds that transplant surgeons call those without helmets, "donors on wheels”. But a book on 'Risk’ by my colleague John Adams has made me re-examine my 1convictions . Adams has completely 2undermined my confidence in these apparently sensible precautions. What he has persuasively argued, particularly in relation to seat belts, is that the evidence that they do what they are supposed to do is very suspect. This is 3in spite of numerous claims that seat belts save many thousands of lives every year. There is remarkable data from the year 1970 to 1978 in which countries with wearing of seat belts 4compulsory have had on average about 5 per cent more road accident deaths following the introduction of the law. In the UK, road deaths have decreased steadily from about 7,000 a year in 1972 to just over 4,000 in 1989. There is no evidence in the trend for any effect of the seat belt law that was introduced in 1983. Moreover, there is evidence that the number of cyclists and pedestrians killed actually increased by about 10 per cent. 3 because_of what_if even_though in_spite_of imperative compelling compulsory acceptable
空格 1convictions decisions / discriminations / convictions / prejudices
語意後文「undermined my confidence in these apparently sensible precautions」呼應「動搖我的信念」，convictions（信念/堅信）最貼切。
搭配前文用「I am convinced」呼應名詞 convictions，同字根語意連貫。
✗ decisions 是「決定」、prejudices 是「偏見」、discriminations 是「歧視/辨別」，都不符「對安全措施的堅信」語意。
空格 2undermined underwriten / undermined / underlined / undertaken
語意後句「undermined my confidence」指削弱信心，undermine（暗中破壞/削弱）語意正確。
✗ underlined 是「強調」、undertaken 是「著手」、underwriten 拼字錯誤且為「承保」，皆與「削弱信心」相反或不通。
空格 3in spite of because of / what if / even though / in spite of
搭配in spite of + 名詞片語（numerous claims），後接名詞而非子句，符合介系詞片語用法。
語意前句說證據可疑，與「無數宣稱安全帶救命」形成讓步轉折，in spite of（儘管）表讓步。
✗ because of 表原因、even though 須接子句不接名詞、what if 是假設問句，皆不合此處讓步+名詞結構。
空格 4compulsory imperative / compelling / compulsory / acceptable
語意「countries with wearing of seat belts compulsory」指強制配戴，compulsory（強制的）符合法律規定語境。
搭配後文「introduction of the law」呼應「法律強制」，compulsory 與法律搭配自然。
✗ imperative 偏「迫切必要」、compelling 是「有說服力的」、acceptable 是「可接受的」，都非「法律強制」之意。
183.Daniel Harris (丹尼爾·哈裏斯)5 格#080185
Daniel Harris, a scholar of consumption and style, has observed that until photography finally 1supplanted illustration as the “primary means of advertising clothing” in the 1950s, glamour inhered 2less in the face of the drawing, which was by necessity schematic and generalized, than in the sketch’s attitude, posture, and gestures, especially in the strangely dainty positions of the hands. Glamour once resided so emphatically in the stance of the model that the 3faces in the illustrations Glamour once resided so emphatically in the stance of the model that the faces in the illustrations cannot really be said to have 4expressions at all, but angles or tilts. The chin raised upwards in a haughty look; the eyes lowered in an attitude of introspection; the head cocked at an inquisitive or haughty look; the eyes lowered in an attitude of introspection; the head cocked at an inquisitive or coquettish angle: or the profile presented in sharp outline, emanating power the severity like an emperor’s bust 5embossed on a Roman coin. emperor’s bust embossed on a Roman coin. more less merely few readers carriages faces postures faces passion violence expressions
空格 1supplanted supplanted / surpassed / transplanted / implanted
搭配supplant A as（取代 A 成為…）固定搭配，photography supplanted illustration
語意until photography ___ illustration 指攝影「取代」插畫
✗ surpassed 後不接 as；transplanted/implanted 為移植，語意不合
空格 2less more / less / merely / few
搭配less...than 比較結構，glamour inhered ___ in...than in 固定句式
語意魅力較少在臉、較多在姿態，與後文 than in the sketch's attitude 對比
✗ more 與 than 後語意對比顛倒；merely/few 不成 ___...than 結構
空格 3faces readers / carriages / faces / postures
語意the ___ in the illustrations cannot...have expressions 指插畫中的「臉」沒有表情
單複數複數 faces 與後接 cannot...have expressions 一致
✗ readers/carriages 語意不合；postures 與後文 expressions 邏輯不通
空格 4expressions faces / passion / violence / expressions
搭配have expressions（有表情）固定搭配，與 but angles or tilts 對比
語意faces...cannot be said to have ___ 指臉沒有「表情」只有角度
✗ faces/passion/violence 與「臉沒有___，只有角度」的對比邏輯不通
空格 5embossed carved / embalmed / embossed / embraced
搭配embossed on a coin（壓印於錢幣上）固定搭配
語意like an emperor's bust ___ on a Roman coin 指浮雕「壓印」在硬幣上
✗ carved 偏雕刻木石；embalmed 為防腐；embraced 為擁抱，皆不合
184.When to revise? (何時複習？)3 格#080186
Timing is important for revision. Have you noticed that during the school day you get times when you just don't care any longer? I don't mean the lessons you don't like, but the ones you find usually find just don't care any longer? I don't mean the lessons you don't like, but the ones you find usually find OK, but on some occasions, you just can't be bothered with it. You 1may have other things on your mind, be tired, restless or looking forward to what comes next. Whatever the reason, that particular mind, be tired, restless or looking forward to what comes next. Whatever the reason, that particular lesson doesn't get 100 percent 2effort from you. The same is true of revision. Your mental and physical 3attitude is important. If you try to revise when you are tired or totally occupied with something else, attitude is important. If you try to revise when you are tired or totally occupied with something else, your revision will be inefficient and just about worthless. If you approach it feeling fresh, alert and happy, it will be so much easier, and you will learn more, faster. However, if you make no plans and just happy, it will be so much easier, and you will learn more, faster. However, if you make no plans and just slip in a little bit of revision when you feel like it, you probably won’t do much revision! You need a revision timetable, so you don't keep putting it off . revision timetable, so you don't keep putting it off . effort impact collision effect health attitude motivation sentiment putting_it_off giving_it_up letting_it_down throwing_it_away 4 putting_it_off giving_it_up letting_it_down throwing_it_away
空格 1may may / barely / never
時態you ___ have other things on your mind 需助動詞，may（可能）表推測
語意列舉各種可能原因（累、坐不住），語氣為「可能」
✗ barely/never 為否定，與後文列舉多種原因矛盾
空格 2effort effort / impact / collision / effect
搭配固定搭配 100 percent effort（百分之百的努力）
語意lesson doesn't get 100 percent ___ from you 指未投入全部「努力」
✗ impact/collision/effect 與「投入努力」語意不合
空格 3attitude health / attitude / motivation / sentiment
搭配mental and physical ___ 指身心「態度」，attitude 固定搭配
語意後文 If you approach it feeling fresh 呼應心態/態度
✗ health 偏健康；motivation 偏動機；sentiment 偏情感，皆不如 attitude 貼切
185.Cells (細胞)2 格#080187
A cell is the smallest 1unit of structure and function. Thus, cells are the basic building blocks of all organisms. Cells vary in size. With few exceptions, individual cells are 2so small that cannot be seen organisms. Cells vary in size. With few exceptions, individual cells are so small that cannot be seen unaided. In 1665, a British scientist named Robert Hooke observed cells for the first time using a microscope. A microscope is an instrument that magnifies an object. Most images of cells are taken microscope. A microscope is an instrument that magnifies an object. Most images of cells are taken with a microscope and are called micrographs. 3 for_the_first_time for_the_earliest_time at_the_beginning in_the_end
空格 1unit unit / individual / substance / source
搭配固定搭配 the smallest unit of structure and function（最小單位）
語意後句 basic building blocks 呼應「單位」概念
✗ individual/substance/source 與「最小___」語意不合
空格 2so quite / very / so / really
搭配so...that 結構，so small that cannot be seen（如此小以致…）
時態搭配後接 that 子句的結果句
✗ quite/very/really 後不接 that 構成結果句
186.Private Schools in UK (英國私立學校)5 格#080188
Private schools in the UK are redoubling their marketing efforts to foreigners. Almost a third of the 68,000 boarding pupils at such schools 1already come from overseas. But now, with many UK residents 2unwilling or unable to afford the fee-top boarding schools 3edging towards £30,000 ($49,759) a year- and a cultural 4shift away from boarding, many schools are looking abroad to survive. Overseas and a cultural shift away from boarding, many schools are looking abroad to survive. Overseas students now 5account for about….
空格 1already merely / already / yet / never
搭配already come from overseas（早已來自海外）固定搭配，肯定句用 already
語意Almost a third...___ come from overseas 指現況「已經」如此
✗ merely 語意弱；yet/never 多用於否定/疑問，與肯定陳述不合
空格 2unwilling eager / incompetent / unwilling / incapable
搭配unwilling or unable to afford（不願或無法負擔）固定並列，與後接 unable 對稱
語意residents ___ or unable to afford the fee 指「不願」付高學費
✗ eager 與「逃離寄宿」矛盾；incompetent/incapable 多接 of，且語意重複 unable
空格 3edging touching / edging / declining / raising
搭配edging towards（逐漸逼近某數字）固定搭配，後接 towards £30,000
語意學費「逐步逼近」三萬鎊
✗ touching 不接 towards；declining 為下降矛盾；raising 為及物動詞，缺受詞
空格 4shift transfer / alter / switch / shift
搭配固定搭配 a cultural shift away from（文化上的轉變）
詞性a cultural ___ away from 需名詞，shift 可作名詞
✗ transfer/alter/switch 與 cultural...away from 不慣用，且 alter 多作動詞
空格 5account count / account / distribute / portion
搭配account for（占…比例）固定片語動詞，後接 for about
語意Overseas students now ___ for about 指海外生「占」一定比例
✗ count 須 count for 不慣用此義；distribute/portion 與「占比例」語意不合
187.Charity (慈善機構)3 格#080189
Of the $260.28 billion given to charities in 2005, 76.5 percent came from individual 1donors . These Of the $260.28 billion given to charities in 2005, 76.5 percent came from individual donors . These individuals gave to a wide range of nonprofit institutions, from museums to hospitals to religious organizations, with a major 2emphasis on disaster relief in the aftermath of the Asian tsunami. In 2005, organizations, with a major emphasis on disaster relief in the aftermath of the Asian tsunami. In 2005, Americans gave a total of 2.2 percent of their household income, slightly above the 40-year 3average level of 2.1 percent. level of 2.1 percent. distress address emphasis emphasize even regular average equal
空格 1donors donates / donors / donations / contributions
詞性from individual ___ 需名詞，且指「捐款人」，donors（捐贈者）
語意後句 These individuals gave 回指這些「捐贈者」
✗ donates 為動詞；donations/contributions 指捐款本身，但 from individual 指「人」較合，且後文 individuals 呼應 donors
空格 2emphasis distress / address / emphasis / emphasize
搭配固定搭配 with a major emphasis on（特別著重於）
詞性a major ___ on 需名詞；emphasize 為動詞不可填
✗ emphasize 詞性錯；distress/address 與 a major ___ on 語意不合
空格 3average even / regular / average / equal
搭配固定搭配 the 40-year average level（四十年平均水準）
語意slightly above the 40-year ___ level 指略高於「平均」
✗ even/regular/equal 與「四十年平均水準」語意不合
188.Carbon Detox (碳排毒)4 格#080190
In his 1fascinating book Carbon Detox, George Marshall argues that people are not persuaded by information. Our views are formed by the views of the people with whom we mix. Of the 2narratives that might penetrate these circles, we are more likely to listen to those that offer us some reward. A story that tells us that the world is cooking and that we'll have to make sacrifices for the sake of future generations is less likely to be accepted than the more rewarding idea that climate change is a conspiracy hatched by scheming governments and venal scientists, and that strong, independent- minded people should unite to defend their freedoms. He proposes that instead of arguing for sacrifice, 3environmentalists should show where the rewards might lie: that understanding what the science is saying and planning accordingly is the smart thing to do, which will protect your interests more effectively than flinging abuse at scientists. We should 4emphasize the old-fashioned virtues of uniting in the face of a crisis, of resourcefulness and community action. Projects like the transition town’s network and proposals for a green new deal tell a story which people are more willing to hear. discard abandon emphasize elaborate
空格 1fascinating fascinating / assuming / enduring / discriminating
詞性his ___ book 需形容詞修飾 book；fascinating（引人入勝的）
語意正向描述書本，與後文論點鋪陳一致
✗ assuming/enduring/discriminating 形容書語意不合
空格 2narratives chronicles / narratives / commentaries / annotations
語意Of the ___ that might penetrate these circles 指能滲入圈子的「敘事」，後文 A story 呼應
單複數複數 narratives 與 those that offer 一致
✗ chronicles 編年史；commentaries 評論；annotations 註解，皆語意較窄不合
空格 3environmentalists environmentalists / scientists / narrators / organizers
語意instead of arguing for sacrifice, ___ should show rewards 指「環保人士」應改變策略，與全文氣候主題一致
單複數複數 environmentalists 對應 should show 主詞
✗ scientists/narrators/organizers 與主張改變論述策略的主體不符
空格 4emphasize discard / abandon / emphasize / elaborate
時態We should ___ 助動詞後接原形動詞
語意should ___ the old-fashioned virtues 指應「強調」傳統美德
✗ discard/abandon 為捨棄，與 virtues 語意相反；elaborate 為詳述，不貼切
189.Emerald (祖母綠)6 格#080191
Emerald is defined by its green color. To be an emerald, a specimen must have a 1distinctly green color that falls in the range from bluish green to green to slightly yellowish green. To be an emerald, the specimen must also have a rich color. Stones with weak saturation or light tone should be called “green beryl.” 2IfIf the beryl’s color is greenish blue, then it is an “aquamarine.” If it is greenish yellow, it is “heliodor”. This color definition is a source of 3confusion . Which hue, tone, and saturation yellow, it is “heliodor”. This color definition is a source of confusion . Which hue, tone, and saturation combinations are the dividing lines between "green beryl" and "emerald"? Professionals in the gem and jewellery trade can disagree on where the lines should be 4drawn . Some believe that the name and jewellery trade can disagree on where the lines should be drawn . Some believe that the name "emerald" should be used when chromium is the cause of the green color, and that stones colored by vanadium should be called "green beryl." Calling a gem an "emerald" instead of a "green beryl" can vanadium should be called "green beryl." Calling a gem an "emerald" instead of a "green beryl" can have a significant 5impact upon its price and marketability. This "color 6confusion " exists within the United States. In some other countries, any beryl with a green color-no matter how faint-is called an United States. In some other countries, any beryl with a green color-no matter how faint-is called an
空格 1distinctly ambiguously / enigmatically / distinctly / instinctively
詞性___ green color 需副詞修飾 green；distinctly（明顯地）
語意a distinctly green color 指顏色「明顯」呈綠，與後文 rich color 呼應
✗ ambiguously/enigmatically 為含糊；instinctively 為本能地，皆語意不合
空格 2IfIf Even / IfIf / Although / As
時態___ the beryl's color is greenish blue, then it is 條件句需 If...then 結構（原檔誤植成 IfIf）
語意與後句 If it is greenish yellow 平行的條件句
✗ Even/Although/As 不構成 If...then 條件結果句；選項僅 IfIf 可成立
空格 3confusion conclusion / inspiration / confusion / arrangement
語意a source of ___，後文問哪裡是分界、專家也意見不一，指「混淆」之源
搭配source of confusion 固定搭配
✗ conclusion/inspiration/arrangement 與「分界不明、各執一詞」語意不合
空格 4drawn located / drawn / picked / chosen
搭配draw the line（劃界線）固定搭配，被動 where the lines should be drawn
時態should be ___ 被動需過去分詞
✗ located/picked/chosen 與 lines（界線）搭配不慣用
空格 5impact meaning / affect / impact / role
搭配have a significant impact upon（對…有重大影響）固定搭配，後接 upon
詞性a significant ___ 需名詞；affect 為動詞不可填
✗ affect 為動詞；meaning/role 與 significant ___ upon price 語意不合
空格 6confusion confusion / definition / clarification / provision
語意This "color ___" 回指前文同主題，引號內呼應 color confusion
搭配color confusion（顏色混淆）為文中反覆出現用語
✗ definition/clarification/provision 與引號回指「混淆」主題不符
190.APS (澳大利亞心理學會)5 格#080192
The APS supports the development of an Australian curriculum for psychological science. The APS The APS supports the development of an Australian curriculum for psychological science. The APS Division of Psychological Research, Education and Training, in 1consultation with teacher and curriculum representatives from every State and Territory in Australia, 2has developed a proposed framework for representatives from every State and Territory in Australia, has developed a proposed framework for senior secondary school studies in psychological science. This framework 3is modelled on the current senior science curricula that were developed and published by the Australian Curriculum, Assessment senior science curricula that were developed and published by the Australian Curriculum, Assessment and Reporting Authority. The APS hopes that this framework will 4facilitate a dialogue between educators and their local curriculum authority, with the aim of working towards a more 5consistent educators and their local curriculum authority, with the aim of working towards a more consistent approach to the teaching of psychological science at secondary school level and optimising the preparation for students going on to undergraduate psychology studies at university, as well as the preparation for students going on to undergraduate psychology studies at university, as well as the effective use of psychological principles in everyday life. 2 been_developed has_developed developing had_developed 3 is_modelled_on is_relied_on related_with is_modelled_with
空格 1consultation parliament / consultation / related / partner
搭配固定片語 in consultation with（與⋯協商），介系詞 in + consultation + with 為慣用搭配。
✗ parliament（議會）、related、partner 放入 in ___ with 皆不成固定片語，語意也不通。
空格 2has developed been developed / has developed / developing / had developed
時態主詞 The Division（單數）+ 現在完成式表已完成的動作，has developed 主動式正確。
單複數主詞單數須配 has 而非 have。
✗ been developed 缺助動詞、developing 非完整時態、had developed 過去完成無對應過去時間點。
空格 3is modelled on is modelled on / is relied on / related with / is modelled with
搭配固定搭配 be modelled on（以⋯為範本），介系詞用 on 而非 with。
✗ is relied on 語意是「被依賴」、related with 搭配錯誤、is modelled with 介系詞錯，皆不通。
空格 4facilitate hinder / facilitate / obstruct / release
語意「facilitate a dialogue between educators」指促進對話，facilitate（促進）符合正面意圖。
✗ hinder/obstruct 皆為「阻礙」，與促進對話相反；release（釋放）語意不合。
空格 5consistent resistant / consistent / resistible / persistent
搭配a more consistent approach（更一致的做法），consistent 與 approach 搭配自然。
語意全文談跨州統一課綱，consistent（一致的）符合「一致做法」語意。
✗ resistant/resistible 與「抵抗」有關、persistent 是「持續的」，皆不符「一致」之意。
191.Brain Action (大腦動作)5 格#080193
Researchers in Europe and the US wanted to find out exactly what happens to our brain when we find Researchers in Europe and the US wanted to find out exactly what happens to our brain when we find ourselves stunned with fright in the hope of better understanding how fear interplays with human anxiety disorders. For the first time, they traced and linked three parts of the brain 1responsible for anxiety disorders. For the first time, they traced and linked three parts of the brain responsible for freezing behaviours: the amygdala, ventrolateral periaqueductal grey region and magnocellular nucleus. Mice are excellent lab animals where it comes to anxiety and fear experiments. When a mouse nucleus. Mice are excellent lab animals where it comes to anxiety and fear experiments. When a mouse is scared, its defensive behaviours range from freezing, attacking, risk 2assessment or fleeing the scene. How a mouse acts depends on variables such as access to escape routes or the level of threat faced. So How a mouse acts depends on variables such as access to escape routes or the level of threat faced. So Andreas Lüthi at the Friedrich Miescher Institute for Biomedical Research in Switzerland and colleagues from Europe and the US observed brain activity in mice placed in frightening situations to 3trace the from Europe and the US observed brain activity in mice placed in frightening situations to trace the brain circuits 4responsible for freezing behaviours. In particular, the researchers wanted to learn more about a part of the brain called the ventrolateral periaqueductal grey region, which was believed to about a part of the brain called the ventrolateral periaqueductal grey region, which was believed to 5play some part in a mouse’s instinct to freeze or flee.
空格 1responsible keen / responsible / managed / charged
搭配responsible for（負責…）固定搭配，後接 for freezing behaviours
詞性three parts...___ for 需形容詞修飾名詞
✗ keen 接 on；managed/charged 後接 for 語意不如 responsible 貼切
空格 2assessment assessment / judgment / determination / criticism
搭配固定搭配 risk assessment（風險評估）
語意與 freezing、attacking、fleeing 並列為防禦行為，risk ___ 指評估
✗ judgment/determination/criticism 不與 risk 構成慣用術語
空格 3trace locate / detect / trace / chase
時態to ___ the brain circuits 不定詞需原形，與後文 to trace the brain circuits 重複呼應
搭配trace circuits（追蹤神經迴路）固定搭配
✗ locate/detect 偏定位偵測；chase 為追逐，與 circuits 語意不合
空格 4responsible responsible / liable / responsibility / culpable
搭配circuits ___ for freezing behaviours，responsible for 固定搭配
詞性需形容詞；responsibility 為名詞不可填
✗ responsibility 詞性錯；liable/culpable 多指法律過失，語意不合
空格 5play contribute / play / take / act
搭配固定搭配 play a part in（在…中扮演角色）
時態to ___ some part 不定詞接原形動詞
✗ contribute/act 不接 a part；take part 須 take part in 無冠詞 some part 不慣用
192.Golden Gate Bridge (金門大橋)5 格#080195
San Francisco’s Golden Gate Bridge, a stunning technological and artistic achievement, 1opens to the San Francisco’s Golden Gate Bridge, a stunning technological and artistic achievement, opens to the public after five years of construction. On opening day-“Pedestrian Day”-some 200,000 bridge walkers 2marveled at the 4,200-foot-long suspension bridge, which spans the Golden Gate Strait at the walkers marveled at the 4,200-foot-long suspension bridge, which spans the Golden Gate Strait at the entrance to San Francisco Bay and 3connects San Francisco and Marin County. On May 28, the Golden Gate Bridge opened to vehicular traffic. On May 27, 1937, the Golden Gate Bridge was opened to great Gate Bridge opened to vehicular traffic. On May 27, 1937, the Golden Gate Bridge was opened to great acclaim, a 4symbol of progress in the Bay Area during a time of economic crisis. At 4,200 feet, it was the longest bridge in the world 5until the completion of New York City’s Verrazano-Narrows Bridge in longest bridge in the world until the completion of New York City’s Verrazano-Narrows Bridge in 1964. Today, the Golden Gate Bridge remains one of the world’s most recognizable architectural marveled shocked awestrucked stunned connecting connects connected with_connection symbol mark badge emblem
空格 1opens releases / publishes / opens / discloses
時態敘事性現在式 the Golden Gate Bridge...___ to the public，單數主詞用 opens
搭配open to the public（對公眾開放）固定搭配
✗ releases/publishes/discloses 與「橋對外開放」語意不合
空格 2marveled marveled / shocked / awestrucked / stunned
搭配marvel at（驚嘆於）固定搭配，後接 at the suspension bridge
時態需可接 at 的不及物動詞過去式
✗ shocked/stunned 多用被動且偏震驚；awestrucked 非正確拼法
空格 3connects connecting / connects / connected
時態which spans...and ___ 與 spans 並列，主詞 bridge 第三人稱單數用 connects
單複數對齊前一動詞 spans 的單數現在式
✗ connecting/connected 非與 spans 並列的單數現在式形式
空格 4symbol symbol / mark / badge / emblem
搭配a symbol of progress（進步的象徵）固定搭配
語意in a time of economic crisis 呼應大橋為「象徵」
✗ mark/badge/emblem 與 of progress 不慣用、語意較窄
空格 5until within / after / unless / until
搭配the longest...until the completion of（直到…完工為止）固定搭配
語意保持最長橋直到 1964 年另一橋完工
✗ within/after/unless 與「直到…為止」的時間語意不合
193.Natural Capital (自然資本)5 格#080196
Capital has often been thought of narrowly as physical capital – the machines, tools, and equipment used in the production of other goods, but our wealth and wellbeing also 1relies on natural capital. If used in the production of other goods, but our wealth and wellbeing also relies on natural capital. If we forget this, we risk degrading the services that natural ecosystems provide, which 2support our economies and sustain our lives. These services include purifying our water, 3regulating our climate, economies and sustain our lives. These services include purifying our water, regulating our climate, reducing flood risk, and pollinating our crops. The Natural Capital Project一 a partnership 4among WWF,The Nature Conservancy,University of Minnesota and Stanford University- works to provide WWF,The Nature Conservancy,University of Minnesota and Stanford University- works to provide decision makers with 5reliable ways to assess the true value of the services that ecosystems provide. An essential element of the Natural Capital Project is developing tools that help decision makers protect essential element of the Natural Capital Project is developing tools that help decision makers protect biodiversity and ecosystem services. 1 relies_on contributes_to subject_to attracts_to
空格 1relies on relies on / contributes to / subject to / attracts to
搭配固定片語 rely on（依賴），與前文「relies on natural capital」重複呼應。
時態主詞 wealth and wellbeing 視為整體單數概念，relies on 第三人稱單數正確。
✗ contributes to 是「貢獻於」方向相反、subject to 與 attracts to 皆非「依賴」搭配。
空格 2support supporting / support / supported / supports
單複數關係子句先行詞 the services（複數），which support 動詞用原形複數 support。
時態與並列動詞 sustain 同為原形，主動現在式一致。
✗ supports 為單數動詞與複數先行詞不合、supporting/supported 非限定動詞無法當子句主要動詞。
空格 3regulating guilding / regulating / denying / diminishing
語意「regulating our climate」指調節氣候，與並列的 purifying water、reducing flood risk 同屬生態服務功能。
時態與前後 purifying、reducing 並列，須用 V-ing 動名詞形式一致。
✗ denying（否認）、diminishing（減少）語意不合生態服務；guilding 為拼字錯誤。
空格 4among between / upon / for / among
搭配a partnership among + 三者以上（WWF、TNC、兩所大學），三方以上用 among 而非 between。
✗ between 通常用於兩者、upon/for 與 partnership 搭配不通。
空格 5reliable liable / strong / powerful / reliable
詞性修飾名詞 ways 須用形容詞，reliable ways（可靠的方法）。
語意「reliable ways to assess the true value」指可靠的評估方法，符合語意。
✗ liable（有責任的/易⋯的）語意不合、strong/powerful 修飾 ways 搭配不自然。
194.Effective Leader (有效的領導者)5 格#080197
In search of lessons to 1apply in our own careers, we often try to 2emulate what effective leaders do. In search of lessons to apply in our own careers, we often try to emulate what effective leaders do. Roger Martin says this focus is misplaced, because moves that work in one context may make little sense in another. A more productive, though more difficult, approach is to look at how such leaders sense in another. A more productive, though more difficult, approach is to look at how such leaders 3think . After extensive interviews with more than 50 of them, the author discovered that most are 4integrative thinkers-that is, they can hold in their heads two opposing ideas at once and then come up integrative thinkers-that is, they can hold in their heads two opposing ideas at once and then come up with a new idea that contains elements of 5each but is superior to both.
空格 1apply disclose / appeal / apply / recover
搭配固定搭配 apply...in our own careers（運用於自身職涯），lessons to apply
語意In search of lessons to ___ 指找尋可「應用」的經驗
✗ disclose/appeal/recover 與「運用經驗教訓」語意不合
空格 2emulate collude / conspire / emulate / purge
語意try to ___ what effective leaders do 指「效法」有效領導者的作為
搭配emulate leaders（仿效領導者）固定搭配
✗ collude/conspire 為共謀；purge 為清除，皆語意不合
空格 3think launch / behave / perform / think
語意look at how such leaders ___，與後文 integrative thinkers 呼應，指看其如何「思考」
搭配how leaders think 與 thinkers 同源呼應
✗ launch/behave/perform 與後文 integrative thinkers（思考者）邏輯不接
空格 4integrative integrative / indispensable / accountable / dependable
語意most are ___ thinkers-that is, they can hold...two opposing ideas，後接定義指「整合型」思考者
搭配integrative thinkers（整合型思考者）固定搭配
✗ indispensable/accountable/dependable 與「能整合對立想法」的定義不符
空格 5each all / each / individual / which
語意contains elements of ___ 指含「兩者各自」的元素，呼應 two opposing ideas
單複數指兩個對立想法中的「每一個」，用 each
✗ all 指三者以上；individual/which 在此語法/語意不通

Sydney is becoming eﬀective in making the best of its limited available unconstrained land. Sydney is Sydney is becoming eﬀective in making the best of its limited available unconstrained land. Sydney is suitable for integrating suitable business, office, residential, retail and other development in accessible locations so as to maximise public transport 1patronage and encourage walking and cycling. Also this locations so as to maximise public transport patronage and encourage walking and cycling. Also this city can reduce the 2consumption of land for housing and associated urban development on the urban fringe. For the proposed mixed business, mixeduse and business park areas, there was no employment fringe. For the proposed mixed business, mixeduse and business park areas, there was no employment data available for 3comparable areas. It is also concluded that lack of housing supply will affect corruption degradation consumption descent competent comparable compatible consistent 4affordability prices industry sector
空格 1patronage verbiage / patronage / visage / pillage
搭配固定搭配 public transport patronage（大眾運輸搭乘量/使用率）
語意maximise public transport ___ 指提高運輸「搭乘量」
✗ verbiage/visage/pillage 與大眾運輸語意完全不合
空格 2consumption corruption / degradation / consumption / descent
搭配固定搭配 the consumption of land（土地的使用/消耗）
語意reduce the ___ of land for housing 指減少土地「消耗」
✗ corruption/degradation/descent 與「減少土地消耗」語意不合
空格 3comparable competent / comparable / compatible / consistent
詞性for ___ areas 需形容詞修飾 areas；comparable（可比較的）
語意no employment data available for ___ areas 指「可比較的」地區
✗ competent/compatible/consistent 與「可比較地區」語意不合
空格 4affordability affordability / prices / industry / sector
搭配固定搭配 housing affordability（住房負擔能力）
語意lack of housing supply will affect ___ 指影響住房「可負擔性」
✗ prices/industry/sector 與「供給不足影響可負擔性」的因果語意較弱
196.The decline in marriage rates (結婚率的下降)4 格#080199
The 1decline in marriage rates and increase in divorce rates has led to a 2decrease in the proportion of the population that is formally married. In 1986, 60% of the population aged 15 years and over were the population that is formally married. In 1986, 60% of the population aged 15 years and over were married; by 2001 this proportion had decreased to 55%. 3Conversely , the proportion of the population aged 15 years and over who were never married 4increased from 29% in 1986 to 32% in 2001. At the aged 15 years and over who were never married increased from 29% in 1986 to 32% in 2001. At the same time, the proportion of the population who were divorced increased, from 5% in 1986 to 7% in 2001, while the proportion of the population who were widowed remained at around 6%. 2001, while the proportion of the population who were widowed remained at around 6%. Conclusively Conversely Consequently Coincidently declined increased remained balanced
空格 1decline peak / decline / increase / growth
語意The ___ in marriage rates 與 increase in divorce 對比，並導致 decrease in married proportion，指結婚率「下降」
搭配decline in rates（比率下降）固定搭配
✗ peak/increase/growth 與後文「已婚比例下降」的因果矛盾
空格 2decrease loss / decrease / increase / growth
語意led to a ___ in the proportion that is married，數據由 60% 降到 55%，指「減少」
搭配a decrease in the proportion 固定搭配
✗ loss 不搭 proportion；increase/growth 與 60%→55% 下降矛盾
空格 3Conversely Conclusively / Conversely / Consequently / Coincidently
語意前句已婚比例下降、本句未婚比例上升，需轉折詞 Conversely（相反地）
詞性句首連接副詞引導對比
✗ Consequently 表結果；Conclusively/Coincidently 與前後對比關係不符
空格 4increased declined / increased / remained / balanced
語意the proportion...divorced ___, from 5% to 7%，數據上升指「增加」
時態過去式 increased 與全段 1986→2001 時態一致
✗ declined/remained/balanced 與 5%→7% 的上升數據矛盾
197.Northern Hemisphere (北半球)5 格#080200
The increasing darkness in the Northern Hemisphere this time of year indicates to the plant that 1fall is coming on. So it starts recouping materials from the 2leaves before they drop off. Evergreens protect their needle-like foliage from freezing with 3waxy coatings and natural "antifreezes". But broadleaf plants, like sugar maples, birches, and sumacs, have no such protections. As a result, they 4shed their leaves. But before they do, the plants first try to 5salvage important nutrients such as nitrogen and roots leaves trims stems adhesive sticky waxy glutinous radiated survived shed fell
空格 1fall fall / spring / summer / winter
語意indicates...that ___ is coming on，落葉、入冬前的季節為「秋天」fall
搭配fall is coming on（秋天將至）符合北半球落葉語境
✗ spring/summer 與落葉、轉冷矛盾；winter 已是冬非「即將來」
空格 2leaves roots / leaves / trims / stems
語意recouping materials from the ___，後文 shed their leaves 呼應，指從「葉子」回收養分
單複數複數 leaves 與後文 their leaves 一致
✗ roots/trims/stems 與「回收養分後落下」的對象不符
空格 3waxy adhesive / sticky / waxy / glutinous
搭配固定搭配 waxy coatings（蠟質塗層）
語意常綠植物以「蠟質」塗層與天然抗凍劑護葉
✗ adhesive/sticky/glutinous 為黏性，與防凍護葉的蠟質不符
空格 4shed radiated / survived / shed / fell
搭配固定搭配 shed leaves（落葉、脫落葉子）
時態As a result, they ___ their leaves 現在式原形，主詞 they
✗ radiated/survived 語意不合；fell 為不及物，不可接 their leaves
空格 5salvage salvage / dispatch / transmit / transit
語意try to ___ important nutrients 指「搶救、回收」重要養分
搭配salvage nutrients（搶救養分）與前文 recouping materials 呼應
✗ dispatch/transmit/transit 偏傳送運輸，與「回收保留養分」語意不合
198.Disclose Business Emission (披露商業排放)4 格#080201
There is a need to 1disclose business emission. The 2data of emission can be used to test which company causes climate change …… the government 3campaigners and environmental 4invigilators causes climate change …… the government campaigners and environmental invigilators
空格 1disclose disclose / dismiss / discontinue / disable
搭配disclose business emission（揭露商業排放）固定搭配，與標題呼應
語意後文 The data of emission can be used 指需「揭露」排放資料
✗ dismiss/discontinue/disable 與「揭露排放資料」語意相反或不合
空格 2data influence / data / result / damage
語意The ___ of emission can be used to test，指排放「數據」可供檢驗
搭配data of emission（排放數據）與前句 emission 呼應
✗ influence/result/damage 與「可被用來檢驗」的客體不符
空格 3campaigners champions / volunteers / campaigners / pioneer
語意the government ___ and environmental invigilators 並列，指政府「倡議者/活動人士」
單複數複數 campaigners 與 invigilators 並列一致
✗ champions/volunteers 語意較泛；pioneer 為單數，與並列複數不符
空格 4invigilators invigilators / watcher / monitor / obligators
單複數environmental ___ 需複數，與 campaigners 並列；watcher/monitor 為單數
語意指環境「監督者」
✗ watcher/monitor 為單數不對齊；obligators 語意不合
199.Indian Onion (印度洋蔥)5 格#080202
The most 1vital ingredient in Indian cooking, the 2basic element with which all dishes begin and, The most vital ingredient in Indian cooking, the basic element with which all dishes begin and, normally, the cheapest vegetable available, the pink onion is an essential item in the shopping basket of families of all classes. A popular saying holds that you will never starve because you can always of families of all classes. A popular saying holds that you will never starve because you can always afford a roti a piece of simple, flat bread and an onion. But in recent weeks, the onion has started to seem an unaffordable 3luxury for India's poor. Over the past few days, another sharp 4surge in prices has seem an unaffordable luxury for India's poor. Over the past few days, another sharp surge in prices has begun to unsettle the influential urban middle classes. The sudden spike in prices has been caused by large exports to neighboring countries and a shortage of supply. With its capacity for bringing down large exports to neighboring countries and a shortage of supply. With its capacity for bringing down governments and scarring political careers, the onion plays an 5explosive role in Indian politics. This week reports of rising onion prices have made front-page news and absorbed the attention of the week reports of rising onion prices have made front-page news and absorbed the attention of the
空格 1vital vital / impressive / subordinate / inferior
語意句意「最重要的食材」，vital=至關重要的，搭配 ingredient 最貼切
搭配the most + 形容詞 表最高級，vital 為正向強調語氣
✗ impressive(令人印象深刻)語意偏；subordinate(次要)、inferior(較差)與「最重要」相反
空格 2basic base / basic / nonessential / adventitious
詞性後接名詞 element，須用形容詞，basic(基本的)是形容詞
語意the basic element 基本元素，呼應「所有菜都從它開始」
✗ base 多為名詞/動詞；nonessential(非必要)、adventitious(偶然的)語意不合
空格 3luxury economy / luxury / element / ingredients
搭配an unaffordable luxury 買不起的奢侈品，固定說法
語意前文 cheapest 此處轉折，洋蔥變成窮人的「奢侈品」
✗ economy、element、ingredients 與 unaffordable(買不起)搭配不成立
空格 4surge surge / plummet / reduction / fluctuation
搭配a sharp surge in prices 物價急遽上漲，固定搭配
語意後文 spike in prices、explosive 都指漲價，方向一致
✗ plummet/reduction 指下跌與漲價相反；fluctuation(波動)無方向、不搭 sharp
空格 5explosive explosive / fulminant / excitable / expensive
語意搭配前文 bringing down governments，洋蔥扮演「爆炸性/引爆」的政治角色
詞性修飾 role 須用形容詞，explosive 為形容詞
✗ fulminant 太醫學罕用；excitable(易激動的)修飾人；expensive(昂貴)與 role 不搭
200.Civil War (南北戰爭)4 格#080203
A Civil War reenactment is in part a memorial service. It is partly, too, a leisure activity. Furthermore, A Civil War reenactment is in part a memorial service. It is partly, too, a leisure activity. Furthermore, most reenactors assert an educational import to the performance, and to develop their roles, many pursue archival research with a rare dedication. On the other hand, Civil War reenactments are pursue archival research with a rare dedication. On the other hand, Civil War reenactments are increasingly commercial 1spectacles , with as many as fifty thousand Americans routinely gathering at (or near) historical Civil War battlefields in order to stage performances that purport to recreate the (or near) historical Civil War battlefields in order to stage performances that purport to recreate the conflict, while hundreds of thousands more spectate (Hadden 1996:5). I will show how theoretical issues of 2authenticity arise as practical problems in the Civil War reenactment community by presenting my own observations from the 2006 Gettysburg reenactment and by 3relying on a number presenting my own observations from the 2006 Gettysburg reenactment and by relying on a number of texts produced by participant-observers. In particular I will refer to Robert Lee Hodge, who was made famous by Tony Horwitz's 1996 book, Confederates in the Attic, and who served as a kind of made famous by Tony Horwitz's 1996 book, Confederates in the Attic, and who served as a kind of Virgil to the author on his journey through a Confederate Valhalla. From these sources I will 4argue that 'Living History' performances require an interpretive apparatus that takes genuine history as its 'Living History' performances require an interpretive apparatus that takes genuine history as its authority, while remaining external to both participants and tourists, who may well be unaware of how closely their own involvement approximates genuine historical events. closely their own involvement approximates genuine historical events. possibility purity authenticity reliability relying undertaking witnessing reviewing question suppose indicate argue
空格 1spectacles transactions / spectacles / exhibitions / contributions
搭配commercial spectacles 商業性盛大場面，與後文數萬人聚集表演呼應
單複數前面 are 為複數動詞，須複數名詞，spectacles 複數
✗ transactions(交易)、exhibitions(展覽)、contributions(貢獻)語意不如「奇觀表演」貼切
空格 2authenticity possibility / purity / authenticity / reliability
語意全文主題是 authenticity(真實性)，後文 genuine history 反覆呼應
詞性issues of ___ 須名詞，authenticity 為名詞
✗ possibility、purity、reliability 雖皆名詞，但與重演「真實性」主題不符
空格 3relying relying / undertaking / witnessing / reviewing
搭配rely on 固定搭配，後接 on a number of texts
時態by + V-ing 結構，與前面 by presenting 並列
✗ undertaking/witnessing/reviewing 都不接 on，破壞 rely on 搭配
空格 4argue question / suppose / indicate / argue
搭配I will argue that... 提出論點的固定句型
語意後接整段作者論證主張，argue 最合宜
✗ question/suppose/indicate 與「我將論證」的學術論述語氣不如 argue 精準
201.Distance learning (遠程學習)4 格#080204
Distance learning can be highly beneficial to a large variety of people from young students wanting to expand their horizons to adults looking for more job security. with programs that allow learners of all expand their horizons to adults looking for more job security. with programs that allow learners of all ages to take courses for fun, personal advancement and degrees, distance learning can meet the 1needs of a diverse population. Perhaps one of the most notable and often talked about 2advantages of of a diverse population. Perhaps one of the most notable and often talked about advantages of distance learning is the flexibility the majority of programs allow students to learn when and where it's convenient for them. For 3those who are struggling to balance their distance learning goals with convenient for them. For those who are struggling to balance their distance learning goals with working a fulltime job and taking care of a family this kind of flexibility can allow many people to pursue education who would not otherwise be able to do so. 4Since there are no on-campus courses to pursue education who would not otherwise be able to do so. Since there are no on-campus courses to attend, students can learn from their own homes, at work on their lunch breaks and from virtually anywhere with internet access. For some it can even be a big source of savings on the fuel costs and anywhere with internet access. For some it can even be a big source of savings on the fuel costs and time required to commute to classes.
空格 1needs eagerness / desire / needs / expectations
搭配meet the needs of 滿足…的需求，固定搭配
語意後文 diverse population，呼應滿足多元族群需求
✗ eagerness/desire/expectations 與 meet 搭配不如 meet the needs 自然道地
空格 2advantages strengths / advantages / enhancements / developments
單複數前 one of the most ... 須複數名詞，advantages 複數
語意often talked about advantages 常被談論的優點，後文 flexibility 即一例
✗ strengths/enhancements/developments 雖近義，advantages 才是 distance learning 常用搭配
空格 3those individual / person / these / those
搭配those who 那些…的人，固定關係代名詞用法
單複數後接 who are struggling 複數子句，須用 those
✗ individual/person 為單數不接 who are；these 不慣用於 these who 結構
空格 4Since Despite / Since / Nevertheless / Thus
搭配Since + 主詞 + 動詞，引導原因子句
語意因為沒有實體課程，所以能在家學習，符合因果
✗ Despite 後不接子句且為轉折；Nevertheless/Thus 為副詞，語意也不合因果
202.Circular Time (循環的時間)4 格#080205
Each of us is born into and raised in a particular"time world"-- an environment with its own 1rhythm to which we entrain ourselves. Certain cultures have related to time as a 2cyclical phenomenon, in which there is no pressure or future anxiety. The whole of existence goes around: the cycle of the 3seasons, seasons , of planting and harvesting, the daily return of the sun, of birth and death. In circular time there is no pressing 4need to achieve and create newness, or to insatiably produce more than is needed to simply survive. Additionally, there is no fear of death. Such societies have successfully integrated the past and future into a peaceful sense of the present. They also honored the wisdom of elders who held the knowledge of the past-- upon which the future was clearly linked. area shape rhythm inclination
空格 1rhythm area / shape / rhythm / inclination
語意an environment with its own rhythm 有自己的節奏，呼應 entrain(同步)
搭配time world 與 rhythm 主題一致，後文季節循環即一種 rhythm
✗ area/shape/inclination 與「時間世界的節奏」語意不合
空格 2cyclical cyclical / conventional / recycling / cylindrical
詞性修飾 phenomenon 須形容詞，cyclical 為形容詞
語意cyclical phenomenon 循環現象，後文 cycle of seasons、goes around 呼應
✗ conventional(傳統)、recycling(回收)、cylindrical(圓柱狀)語意皆不合循環時間
空格 3seasons days / seasons / arrangement / periods
搭配the cycle of the seasons 四季循環，慣用搭配，與 planting and harvesting 並列
語意後文 daily return of the sun，此處指季節循環
✗ days 與 daily return 重複；arrangement/periods 不搭 cycle of
空格 4need issue / point / cause / need
搭配no pressing need to 沒有迫切需要去…，固定搭配
詞性後接 to achieve，need to 不定詞結構
✗ issue/point/cause 都不接 pressing + to 不定詞，破壞 pressing need 搭配
203.A Bad Title (一個錯誤的標題)5 格#080206
A good story may be given a bad title by its author, and so started toward failure. Novices are peculiarly 1liable to this fault, usually through 2allowing themselves to be too easily satisfied. They go to peculiarly liable to this fault, usually through allowing themselves to be too easily satisfied. They go to 3infinite pains to make the story itself fresh and individual, and then cap it with a 4commonplace phrase that is worse than no title at all. A good title is apt, specific, attractive, new, and short. A title is apt if it that is worse than no title at all. A good title is apt, specific, attractive, new, and short. A title is apt if it is an outgrowth of the plot—a text, as I have said. It stands definitely for that particular story, and gives a suggestion of what is to come—but only a suggestion, lest it should anticipate the denouement and a suggestion of what is to come—but only a suggestion, lest it should anticipate the denouement and so 5satisfy the curiosity of the reader too soon.
空格 1liable apt / liable / keen / responsible
搭配be liable to (this fault) 易犯…的固定搭配，指容易有此毛病
語意Novices(新手)容易犯這毛病，liable to 表傾向
✗ apt 須接 to+原形(apt to do)非 to this fault；keen/responsible 與 to this fault 語意不合
空格 2allowing allowing / allowed / allowance / allow
時態through + V-ing 介系詞後接動名詞，用 allowing
搭配allow themselves to be satisfied 反身結構，allowing 接 themselves
✗ allowed/allow/allowance 不符 through 後須動名詞的文法
空格 3infinite infinite / pleasant / limited / enclosed
搭配go to infinite pains 費盡無數心力，固定搭配
語意與後文「讓故事新鮮獨特」呼應，下無數工夫
✗ pleasant/limited/enclosed 與 take pains 的「費盡心力」語意相反或不搭
空格 4commonplace precious / distinguished / commonplace / eminent
語意commonplace phrase 平庸俗套詞句，與前面 fresh and individual 對比
詞性修飾 phrase 須形容詞，commonplace 為形容詞
✗ precious/distinguished/eminent 皆正面詞，與「比沒標題更糟」的貶義對比不合
空格 5satisfy meet / satisfy / improve / compromise
搭配satisfy the curiosity 滿足好奇心，固定搭配
語意anticipate the denouement and so satisfy... 提前洩底滿足讀者好奇
✗ meet 較少搭 curiosity；improve/compromise 與「滿足好奇心」語意不合
204.History Books (曆史書籍)5 格#080207
History is selective. What history books tell us about the past is not everything that happened, but History is selective. What history books tell us about the past is not everything that happened, but what historians 1have selected . They cannot put in everything: choices have to be made.
