460.Employee Mood(員工情緒)4 格#080474
Research from the Terry College of Business reveals 1becoming a happy, helpful employee takes effort and, eventually, that effort 2erodes the energy needed to do one’s job. It could lead to quiet quitting – the new term for just doing your job but not going above and beyond – or even actual quitting. The more people adjust their moods to be happy, the fewer emotional resources they have 3at the end of the day. That means they are less able to handle challenging tasks and interactions and have a harder time staying on task. Their tank is empty despite being in a good mood, Frank explained. For managers, this means it may make more sense to meet employees 4where they are emotionally and not force upbeat attitudes in the office. For employees, it may mean letting bad days happen and leaving more mood-demanding work — such as sales calls or tough conversations — for better days. keeping becoming become recruiting
空格 1becoming keeping / becoming / become / recruiting
搭配動名詞作 takes 的主詞，becoming a happy employee takes effort「成為快樂員工需花功夫」
詞性句首需動名詞當主詞，becoming 接補語 a happy employee
✗ become 為原形無法當主詞、keeping/recruiting 語意為保持/招募，與「成為快樂員工」不合
空格 2erodes erodes / enhances / boosts / synergies
語意effort erodes the energy「努力消耗精力」，後文 fewer emotional resources、tank is empty 呼應耗損
單複數主詞 that effort 單數，動詞第三人稱單數 erodes
✗ enhances/boosts 語意相反（增強）、synergies 為名詞非動詞，與「耗損精力」矛盾
空格 3at until / at / before / beyond
搭配at the end of the day「在一天結束時」固定介系詞片語
✗ until/before/beyond 不與 the end of the day 構成此固定時間片語，須用 at
空格 4where when / where / as / whom
搭配meet employees where they are「順著員工當下狀態」固定說法，關係副詞 where 引導地點/狀態子句
語意在情緒上順著員工所處的狀態，而非強迫正向
✗ when 表時間、as 表方式/原因、whom 須接人作受詞，皆不合 meet sb where they are 的慣用句型
461.Prisoners(囚犯)4 格#080475
The primary goal for this year-long campaign, founded by the English lawyer Peter Benenson and a small group of writers, academics and lawyers including Quaker peace activist Eric Baker, was to small group of writers, academics and lawyers including Quaker peace activist Eric Baker, was to identify individual prisoners of conscience around the world and then campaign for their release. In early 1962, the campaign had received enough public support to become a permanent organization early 1962, the campaign had received enough public support to become a permanent organization and was 1renamed Amnesty International. Under British law, Amnesty International was classed as a political organization and therefore excluded from tax-free charity status. To work around this, the political organization and therefore excluded from tax-free charity status. To work around this, the "Fund for the Persecuted" was established in 1962 to receive donations to support prisoners and their families. The name was later changed to the "Prisoners of Conscience Appeal Fund" and is now a families. The name was later changed to the "Prisoners of Conscience Appeal Fund" and is now a separate and independent charity which provides relief and 2rehabilitation grants to prisoners of conscience in the UK and around the world. Amnesty International has, since its founding, pressured conscience in the UK and around the world. Amnesty International has, since its founding, pressured governments to release those persons it considers to be prisoners of conscience. Governments, conversely, tend to deny that the specific prisoners identified by Amnesty International are, in fact, conversely, tend to deny that the specific prisoners identified by Amnesty International are, in fact, being held on the grounds Amnesty claims; they allege that these prisoners pose 3genuine threats to the security of their countries. The concept of "Prisoners of conscience" became a controversy around the security of their countries. The concept of "Prisoners of conscience" became a controversy around Nelson Mandela's 4imprisonment .
空格 1renamed retained / renamed / rewarded
搭配was renamed Amnesty International「更名為國際特赦組織」，be renamed + 新名稱
語意組織獲足夠支持後改名
✗ retained 為保留、rewarded 為獎賞，與「更名」語意不合
空格 2rehabilitation rehabilitation / engagement / engaging / healing
搭配relief and rehabilitation grants「救濟與復健補助」並列搭配
語意提供良心犯救濟與復原協助
✗ engagement/engaging 偏參與、healing 雖近義但 rehabilitation 為正式「重建/復健」用詞且與 relief 並列自然
空格 3genuine deceptive / genuine / trivial / disguised
搭配genuine threats「真正的威脅」，政府宣稱這些囚犯構成國安實質威脅
語意政府辯稱威脅是真實的，故反駁特赦組織
✗ deceptive/disguised 偏欺騙/偽裝、trivial 為瑣碎，與政府主張「真實重大威脅」矛盾
空格 4imprisonment felon / redemption / imprisonment / emancipation
搭配Nelson Mandela's imprisonment「曼德拉的入獄」，呼應全文 prisoners of conscience 監禁主題
✗ felon 為重刑犯（人）、redemption/emancipation 偏救贖/解放，與「入獄監禁」語意不合
462.Powerless Creatures(無能爲力的生物)3 格#080476
Individual human beings are relatively powerless creatures, no match for lions or bears. It’s what they Individual human beings are relatively powerless creatures, no match for lions or bears. It’s what they can do as groups that has 1enabled them to take over the planet. These groupings – corporations, religions, states – are now part of a vast network of 2interconnected information flows. Finding points of religions, states – are now part of a vast network of interconnected information flows. Finding points of resistance, where smaller units can 3stand up to the waves of information washing around the globe, is becoming harder all the time. becoming harder all the time. rise raise stand race
空格 1enabled embodied / enabled / encouraged / stimulated
搭配enable sb to do「使某人能夠…」固定句型，has enabled them to take over the planet
語意群體合作使人類得以稱霸地球
✗ embodied 為體現、encouraged/stimulated 偏鼓勵/刺激，不如 enable sb to do 表「使能夠」精準
空格 2interconnected overlapping / interconnected / paralleled / dynamic
搭配interconnected information flows「相互連結的資訊流」，呼應 vast network 的網絡意象
✗ overlapping/paralleled/dynamic 偏重疊/平行/動態，不如 interconnected 表「彼此連結成網」
空格 3stand rise / raise / stand / race
搭配stand up to「對抗、抵擋」固定片語動詞，smaller units can stand up to the waves of information
語意前文 points of resistance 呼應「抵抗」資訊浪潮
✗ rise/race 不接 up to 表抵抗、raise 為及物動詞需受詞，stand up to 才合「挺身對抗」
463.Evolution(演變)5 格#080477
A creature may have fine physical defenses such as hard armor or sharp spines. It may have powerful chemical defenses such as an 1appalling smell or a foul taste but none of these defenses is much used in the 2struggle for survival unless the animal also has the right behavior to go with it. Evolution 3shapes a living creature’s size and color, and it also shapes an animal’s actions and behavioral patterns. The most 4automatic behaviors are instinctive or in-built. In other words, the creatures can perform the actions without having to learn what to do it by trial and error. attempt 5trail success certainty
空格 1appalling mysterious / exotic / appalling / ordinary
語意與 foul taste 並列，指化學防禦的負面感官效果，appalling smell（駭人惡臭）語意吻合
✗ mysterious/exotic 偏神秘新奇、ordinary 偏普通，皆不帶防禦所需的『令人嫌惡』語意
空格 2struggle game / competition / struggle / life
搭配固定搭配 the struggle for survival（生存鬥爭），為慣用語
✗ game/competition/life 皆無法構成 struggle for survival 這個固定說法
空格 3shapes shaping / shapes / distorts / distorted
時態主詞 Evolution 為第三人稱單數、現在式陳述通則，需用 shapes
語意與後句 it also shapes 平行重複，正面語意『塑造』而非 distort『扭曲』
✗ shaping 非限定動詞不能當主要動詞、distorts 語意相反、distorted 時態與通則不符
空格 4automatic dangerous / automatic / talented / common
語意下句 instinctive or in-built（本能、內建）對應，最自動的行為即 automatic
✗ dangerous/talented/common 都無法與 instinctive、in-built 形成語意呼應
空格 5trail需覆核 attempt / trail / success / certainty
搭配固定片語 trial and error（嘗試錯誤法），原文拼為 trail 即此搭配的選項對應
語意without having to learn by ___，指不必靠『反覆試錯』學習
✗ attempt/success/certainty 都無法構成 trial-and-error 這個固定學習方式說法
464.Voter(選民)6 格#080478
Researchers have 1discovered that improving voter turnout tends to result in better decision-making — Researchers have discovered that improving voter turnout tends to result in better decision-making — at least amongst fish. A recent study of stickleback found that they select a leader by 2consensus and, the more fish in the pool, the more likely they'll make a good choice. In the experiment, a school of the more fish in the pool, the more likely they'll make a good choice. In the experiment, a school of fish was presented with two mock 3candidates for leader. One of the fake fish swam left, the other right. The pack of real fish was then allowed to decide which leader to follow. 4According to the study, which The pack of real fish was then allowed to decide which leader to follow. According to the study, which was published recently in the journal Current Biology, the stickleback tended to follow the candidate that looked the healthiest. Larger or fatter fish, for example, were usually chosen over smaller fish. Fish that looked the healthiest. Larger or fatter fish, for example, were usually chosen over smaller fish. Fish with spots—often a telltale sign of disease— 5generally lost out to a non-spotty adversary. The fish also seemed to have a preference for a more richly colored candidate, though the researchers say they have seemed to have a preference for a more richly colored candidate, though the researchers say they have yet to determine why. Maybe his 6environmental record won them over?
空格 1discovered disclosed / discovered / unveiled / endorsed
搭配Researchers have discovered that...（研究者發現），為研究報導固定句型
語意後接 that 子句陳述研究結論，用 discovered（發現）最自然
✗ disclosed/unveiled 偏『揭露機密』、endorsed『背書』語意不合研究發現
空格 2consensus需覆核 opinion / forming / decision / making / group / dynamics / consensus / making
搭配固定片語 by consensus（以共識方式），select a leader by consensus 語意完整
詞性介系詞 by 後需名詞，consensus 為單一名詞剛好填入
✗ 其餘為被拆開的兩字組（opinion/forming、decision/making、group/dynamics）無法單格填入並與 by 搭配
空格 3candidates applicants / representatives / candidates / tests
語意下文 mock candidates、the candidate that looked healthiest 重複出現，指選領袖的『候選者』
單複數two mock ___ 需複數，candidates 為複數
✗ applicants/representatives 偏申請者/代表、tests 為測驗，皆與選領袖情境不符且下文未呼應
空格 4According Corresponding / Following / According / Comparing
搭配固定片語 According to the study（根據研究），後接 to + 來源
語意引述研究結論的慣用開頭
✗ Corresponding/Following/Comparing 皆無法與 to 構成『根據…』之引述語意
空格 5generally generally / intensively / exclusively / specifically
語意Fish with spots ___ lost out（帶斑點的魚通常落敗），陳述普遍傾向用 generally
✗ intensively/exclusively/specifically（密集地/唯一地/特定地）皆與『普遍傾向』語氣不合
空格 6environmental health / environmental / selection / physcial
語意結尾 his environmental record won them over? 為政治選舉雙關，呼應 environmental
✗ health/selection/physical 與全文政治選舉戲謔脈絡及『環保政績』雙關不符
465.Micrococcus(微球菌屬)5 格#080479
You've probably noticed that 1synthetic t-shirts stink more after a workout, compared to cotton. But You've probably noticed that synthetic t-shirts stink more after a workout, compared to cotton. But hey—it's not the [fabric's] fault. It's the microbes that hang out on synthetics, that create that 2characteristic stench. That's 3according to a study in the journal Applied and Environmental characteristic stench. That's according to a study in the journal Applied and Environmental Microbiology. Twenty-six volunteers—half men, half women—worked out on spinning bikes for an hour. And they did so outfitted with shirts of cotton, 4polyester , or a cotton/synthetic blend. Then hour. And they did so outfitted with shirts of cotton, polyester , or a cotton/synthetic blend. Then researchers stuffed the sweaty shirts into plastic bags. The next day, a trained panel sniffed them, rating their funk. Unlucky job. Because yes—the polyester shirts were indeed more musty, sour, and rating their funk. Unlucky job. Because yes—the polyester shirts were indeed more musty, sour, and ammonia-like than the cotton. DNA analysis revealed that Micrococcus 5bacteria were to blame. They aren't actually all that common in the armpit itself. And they don't flock to cotton. But researchers say aren't actually all that common in the armpit itself. And they don't flock to cotton. But researchers say they thrive on the open-air lattice of synthetic fibers—where they sit chomping on the long-chain fatty acids in our sweat, turning them into shorter, stinkier molecules. These findings might just explain one acids in our sweat, turning them into shorter, stinkier molecules. These findings might just explain one of the most vexing questions of adolescence: why do stinky shirts smell so unpleasantly different from the body odor in the armpits themselves? Could be because your favorite shirt has a microbiome of its the body odor in the armpits themselves? Could be because your favorite shirt has a microbiome of its
空格 1synthetic fabric / synthetic / organic / antique
語意首句 synthetic t-shirts stink more 已點明，後文 microbes hang out on synthetics 重複
✗ fabric 過泛、organic/antique（有機/古董）與化纖致臭主題無關
空格 2characteristic atypical / characteristic / vigorous / slither
詞性that create that ___ stench，需形容詞修飾 stench，characteristic 作形容詞
語意指『特有的』臭味，characteristic stench 語意吻合
✗ atypical（非典型）語意相反、vigorous 修飾臭味不通、slither 為動詞詞性不符
空格 3according regardless / contrary / according / atypical
搭配固定片語 according to a study（根據一項研究），後接 to
✗ regardless/contrary 需配 of/to 但語意不合、atypical 為形容詞無法構成引述
空格 4polyester silk / wool / polyester / bionic
語意下文 the polyester shirts were indeed more musty 重複出現，且與 cotton 對比
✗ silk/wool 為天然纖維、bionic 非衣料，皆不符實驗中與棉對照的合成纖維
空格 5bacteria molecule / bacteria / fungi / influx
語意Micrococcus ___ were to blame，Micrococcus 為細菌屬，且下文 They thrive on...fibers 指細菌
單複數後接 were 為複數動詞，bacteria 作複數主詞相符
✗ molecule/fungi/influx（分子/真菌/湧入）皆與 Micrococcus 為細菌的事實不符

Every person interested in the topic, whether a student or a hardened expert, will find it indispensable. 3 one_sense it_can_sense you_can_sense it_can_grab
空格 1material report / material / book / journal
搭配「reference material」是固定搭配（參考資料），且後句「it represents extremely good value」承接 material 的價值。
語意本書是參考用工具書，用 reference material 最貼切。
✗ book/journal/report 都不與 reference 構成此處慣用搭配，語意也不如 material 通用。
空格 2assist assist / manage / contribute / attribute
搭配「assist the reader with maps...」是 assist sb with sth 句型。
語意貢獻者投入大量心力是為了「協助」讀者。
✗ contribute/attribute 不接 the reader with；manage 語意不合（非管理讀者）。
空格 3you can sense one sense / it can sense / you can sense / it can grab
語意前文「flicking through the pages」泛指任何讀者翻書，用泛稱的 you 最自然。
詞性需要「主詞+動詞」的完整子句，you can sense 結構正確。
✗ it 指涉不明（無對應單數物）；one sense 缺動詞且生硬。
空格 4highlighting highlighting / highlights / focusing / involves
時態前面已有主要動詞 has，這裡用現在分詞 highlighting 作伴隨／補充說明。
詞性分詞片語修飾前面整句，highlights(動詞原形)會造成兩個並列主動詞。
✗ highlights/involves 為限定動詞，會與 has 衝突；focusing 須接 on，語意也偏窄。
空格 5Every Anyone of / The other / Every / Another
單複數後接單數名詞 person，需用 Every（every+單數名詞）。
語意語意要表達「每一個」對主題有興趣的人都會覺得不可或缺。
✗ Anyone of/Another/The other 與單數 person 及全稱語意不符。
467.Bamboo(竹)5 格#080481
Bamboo is a favoured plant among architects and designers because of its incredible strength and Bamboo is a favoured plant among architects and designers because of its incredible strength and durability. One Colombian architect 1refers to it as nature's steel, but in many respects it is even better than steel: it is lighter and more flexible, and these 2qualities make it the ideal building material in areas than steel: it is lighter and more flexible, and these qualities make it the ideal building material in areas that suffer earthquakes and severe weather patterns. Construction workers in places such as Hong Kong rely on bamboo scaffolding whatever the 3height of the tower block they may be working on: Kong rely on bamboo scaffolding whatever the height of the tower block they may be working on: over a billion people around the world live in a home that is made of bamboo; and China as cultivated the plant for thousands of years. The only drawback to this remarkable product is the cost of the plant for thousands of years. The only drawback to this remarkable product is the cost of transporting it. So for those 5who live in cooler regions of the world, the enormous advantages of this natural building material are less accessible. natural building material are less accessible. talents value qualities abilities top height mass volume has_been_cultivated cultivated was_cultivating had_cultivated 4 has_been_cultivated cultivated was_cultivating had_cultivated
空格 1refers implies / referring / indicates / refers
搭配「refer to it as nature's steel」是 refer to A as B（稱A為B）固定搭配。
時態主詞 architect 為第三人稱單數現在式，用 refers。
✗ referring 非限定動詞、缺主要動詞；implies/indicates 不接 to...as 此結構。
空格 2qualities talents / value / qualities / abilities
語意前句講 lighter、more flexible 等「特性」，these qualities 回指這些性質。
單複數指多項特性，用複數 qualities。
✗ talents/abilities 用於人的才能；value 為抽象單數，無法 these 回指多項。
空格 3height top / height / mass / volume
語意搭鷹架在「大樓的高度」上工作，the height of the tower block 最合理。
搭配「the height of the tower block」是自然搭配（樓高）。
✗ top/mass/volume 與 tower block 搭配及語意都不合（非頂端/質量/體積）。
空格 4has been cultivated需覆核 has been cultivated / cultivated / was cultivating / had cultivated
時態「for thousands of years」搭配現在完成式，表持續至今。
語意植物是被栽種的對象，須用被動 has been cultivated。
✗ cultivated(過去式主動)、was cultivating、had cultivated 都不符「持續至今的被動」語意。
空格 5who are / which / may / who
語意先行詞 those（指人），關係代名詞用 who。
詞性引導關係子句作主詞，who live in...結構正確。
✗ which 用於物；are/may 為動詞，無法引導關係子句。
468.Event Management(事件管理)5 格#080482
Event management is particularly challenging from an operational viewpoint. In many cases, events are staged on sites where everything has been set up over a 24-hour period, with all elements carefully staged on sites where everything has been set up over a 24-hour period, with all elements carefully synchronised . In contrast, many events are years in the planning: large convention bids are often won five years before the event is held. For the very 2competitive bidding process, budgets need to be five years before the event is held. For the very competitive bidding process, budgets need to be developed and prices quoted, requiring a good understanding of market, economic and political trends, as well as consumer choices. This long-term view is the basis of strategic management, which is trends, as well as consumer choices. This long-term view is the basis of strategic management, which is covered in Part 1, and focuses on the event concept, feasibility of the event, legal compliance and financial management. Marketing is a critical 3success factor and other important topic of this first financial management. Marketing is a critical success factor and other important topic of this first section, many events (sporting, cultural and arts) involving long-term sponsorship 4arrangements with key industry players. Relationship building is particularly 5challenging since there are so many key industry players. Relationship building is particularly challenging since there are so many stakeholders involved in events, including government agencies at many levels. Part 1 will look at all these aspects, including strategic risk, before moving on to the second part where operational these aspects, including strategic risk, before moving on to the second part where operational planning and implementation will be covered in detail.
空格 1synchronized explored / operated / synchronized / transmitte
時態with all elements carefully ___，被動分詞構句修飾 elements，用過去分詞 synchronized
語意事件各環節在24小時內被『同步』安排，synchronized 語意吻合
✗ explored/operated（探索/操作）語意不合、transmitte 為錯字且語意不通
空格 2competitive lucrative / competitive / challenging / feasible
語意the very ___ bidding process，搭配 bidding（競標）指『競爭激烈的』投標過程
✗ lucrative/challenging/feasible（有利可圖/困難/可行）皆不及 competitive 直接呼應 bidding 的競爭性
空格 3success sectoral / success / profound / milestone
搭配critical success factor（關鍵成功因素）為商管固定術語
✗ sectoral/profound/milestone 皆無法構成 critical success factor 這個固定詞組
空格 4arrangements settlements / agreements / arranging / arrangements
搭配sponsorship arrangements（贊助安排）為固定搭配
詞性long-term ___ 需名詞，arrangements 為名詞且複數呼應多項贊助
✗ settlements/agreements 語意偏結算/協議、arranging 為動名詞與 sponsorship 名詞並列不順
空格 5challenging challenging / secretive / restrictive / inquisitive
語意Relationship building is particularly ___ since there are so many stakeholders，多方利害關係人使其『困難』
✗ secretive/restrictive/inquisitive（隱密/限制/好問）皆與『因利害關係人眾多而具挑戰性』語意不符
469.Help Other People(幫助他人)5 格#080483
There are many different ways to help other people. Perhaps the most common of these involves There are many different ways to help other people. Perhaps the most common of these involves giving others 1practical help. In our society, there are many individuals who spontaneously help others in this way. Additionally, there are others who belong to organizations which have been set up to in this way. Additionally, there are others who belong to organizations which have been set up to provide help to specific groups, such as the elderly, the disabled, and those with serious physical or 2mental health problems. Most importantly, there are many 3occupations , such as nursing, occupational mental health problems. Most importantly, there are many occupations , such as nursing, occupational therapy and social work, which involve professionals who are trained to provide or organize practical help for others. 4While helping other people in a practical way, many volunteer and professional helpers help for others. While helping other people in a practical way, many volunteer and professional helpers also make use of some counselling skills. These skills can be very useful in enabling people to feel better as described in this book and our book, Counselling Skills in Everyday Life. However, it needs to better as described in this book and our book, Counselling Skills in Everyday Life. However, it needs to be 5recognized that just being able to make use of some counselling skills does not qualify a person as emotional intellectual spiritual mental careers jobs occupations fields Although However While When
空格 1practical practice / practical / professional / perfect
詞性giving others ___ help，需形容詞修飾 help，practical 為形容詞
語意下文 help others in a practical way 重複，指『實際的』幫助
✗ practice 為名詞詞性不符、professional/perfect 與下文 practical way 不呼應
空格 2mental emotional / intellectual / spiritual / mental
搭配physical or mental health problems（生理或心理健康問題）為固定並列搭配
✗ emotional/intellectual/spiritual 雖屬心理面向，但與 physical 對舉時固定用 mental health
空格 3occupations careers / jobs / occupations / fields
語意many ___, such as nursing, occupational therapy and social work，列舉各種『職業』，且下文 occupational 呼應
單複數many 後接複數名詞，occupations 為複數
✗ careers/jobs/fields 雖近義，但與下文 such as nursing... 列舉專業職類及 occupational therapy 用字最貼合的是 occupations
空格 4While Although / However / While / When
時態___ helping other people..., many helpers also...，引導分詞構句『在…的同時』，用 While + V-ing
語意表『一邊提供實際協助、一邊運用諮商技巧』的同時關係
✗ Although 偏讓步、However 為副詞不能引導分詞構句、When 雖可但 While 更貼『同時進行』語意
空格 5recognized recognized / identified / confirmed / established
搭配it needs to be ___ that...（必須被認知到），be recognized that 為固定句型
時態be 後接過去分詞構成被動，recognized 為過去分詞
✗ identified/confirmed/established 皆過去分詞，但與 it needs to be ___ that 子句搭配最自然者為 recognized

This text delivers a thorough and balanced introduction to the Astralian Legal System. It provides a clear grounding in the western and indigenous legal 1traditions , as well as the history of Australian law clear grounding in the western and indigenous legal traditions , as well as the history of Australian law and legal institutions. There is a special emphasis on legal method which forms the 2basis of legal understanding. In particular, the text 3analyses and explains legal method —the following and understanding. In particular, the text analyses and explains legal method —the following and distinguishing of precedent, statutory interpretation and the identification of ratio, which prepares students for their legal study. Numerous quality exercises prepare the way for legal thinking in students for their legal study. Numerous quality exercises prepare the way for legal thinking in students, for example, "Part 3: Legal Method" contains many examples and exercises to 4encourage basis core origin basic analysis analyses analyzing analyzed provoke encourage motivate inspire
空格 1traditions beliefs / heritages / traditions / rituals
搭配legal traditions（法律傳統）為固定搭配，western and indigenous legal traditions 語意完整
單複數並列 western and indigenous 兩類，用複數 traditions
✗ beliefs/heritages/rituals（信念/遺產/儀式）皆無法與 legal 構成『法律傳統』的慣用搭配
空格 2basis basis / core / origin / basic
搭配forms the basis of（構成…的基礎）為固定片語
詞性forms the ___ of，需名詞，basis 為名詞；basic 為形容詞詞性不符
✗ core/origin 為名詞但不構成 form the basis of 慣用語、basic 為形容詞不能接在 the 後當名詞
空格 3analyses analysis / analyses / analyzing / analyzed
時態the text ___ and explains，主詞 the text 第三人稱單數現在式，與 explains 並列需 analyses（動詞第三人稱單數）
✗ analysis 為名詞、analyzing/analyzed 為分詞，皆無法與 explains 構成並列的限定動詞
空格 4encourage provoke / encourage / motivate / inspire
語意exercises to ___ legal thinking，練習用以『鼓勵/促進』法律思考，encourage 最中性貼切
✗ provoke 偏激起負面、motivate/inspire 偏激勵個人動機，與『練習培養法律思考』語感不如 encourage 自然
471.Neutrinos(中微子)4 格#080485
In June 1998, an international team of Japanese and US physicists unveiled strong evidence that elusive subatomic particles known as neutrinos have mass. These findings run 1counter to the standard elusive subatomic particles known as neutrinos have mass. These findings run counter to the standard model of particle physics—the basic theory about the structure of matter—which holds that these electrically neutral, weakly interacting particles have no mass. The discovery means that existing electrically neutral, weakly interacting particles have no mass. The discovery means that existing theoretical models of matter must now be 2revised to include neutrinos with mass. Neutrinos occur in three states: electron, muon, and tau, with the names signifying what is produced when a neutrino three states: electron, muon, and tau, with the names signifying what is produced when a neutrino collides with another particle. 3Observers do not see the neutrinos themselves, but can detect the creation of electrons and muons from faint flashes of light following a particle collision. The physicists creation of electrons and muons from faint flashes of light following a particle collision. The physicists used the giant Super-Kamiokande—the world's biggest neutrino detector buried deep underground in Mozumi, Japan. In the experiment, 4submerged in a 50,000-ton tank of purified water, neutrinos created Mozumi, Japan. In the experiment, submerged in a 50,000-ton tank of purified water, neutrinos created when cosmic rays bombard Earth's upper atmosphere were counted relative to the number expected to penetrate the cavern. The experimenters found that the number of electron-neutrinos detected was to penetrate the cavern. The experimenters found that the number of electron-neutrinos detected was relatively constant with theorized totals, while the number of muon-neutrinos was significantly lower. This indicated that they were disappearing into another state, or "flavor," such as an undetected tau- This indicated that they were disappearing into another state, or "flavor," such as an undetected tau- neutrino, or possibly another type.
空格 1counter opposite / counter / adverse / against
搭配run counter to（與…相牴觸）為固定片語，findings run counter to the standard model 語意完整
✗ opposite/adverse/against 皆無法與 run...to 構成『與…相悖』的慣用語
空格 2revised altered / reversed / revised / removed
語意models must now be ___ to include neutrinos with mass，模型需『修訂』以納入新發現
時態be 後接過去分詞構成被動，revised 為過去分詞
✗ altered/reversed/removed（更改/逆轉/移除）皆過去分詞，但『修訂以納入新資料』語意最貼 revised
空格 3Observers Observers / Scientists / Physicists / Researchers
語意___ do not see the neutrinos themselves, but can detect...，下句 detect 動作對應『觀測者』，且原文重複該字
✗ Scientists/Physicists/Researchers 雖合理，但與下文『看不見微中子本身卻能偵測』的觀測情境最貼切者為 Observers
空格 4submerged submerge / submerging / submerged / submerges
時態neutrinos created..., ___ in a tank，過去分詞作後位修飾『被淹沒於水槽中』，用 submerged
✗ submerge/submerges 為限定動詞、submerging 為主動分詞，皆與『被浸沒』的被動修飾不符
472.Ernest Shackleton(歐內斯特·沙克爾頓)5 格#080486
Ernest Shackleton was a British explorer of the South Pole who is best remembered for leading his Ernest Shackleton was a British explorer of the South Pole who is best remembered for leading his crew to safety after the failed 1expedition of the Endurance (1914–16). Shackleton had been a junior officer on Robert Falcon Scott's discovery expedition (1902–03), and his travels with the Nimrod officer on Robert Falcon Scott's discovery expedition (1902–03), and his travels with the Nimrod (1907-09) had taken him closer to the South Pole than anyone before. After Roald Amundsen reached the Pole in 1911, Shackleton and a crew of 28 men set out in his ship Endurance in 1914, in the 2hope of the Pole in 1911, Shackleton and a crew of 28 men set out in his ship Endurance in 1914, in the hope of being the first to cross the polar continent. The ship was frozen in ice, then crushed, and Shackleton and his men set out in lifeboats after nearly a year and a half on the ice. Shackleton, known as "The and his men set out in lifeboats after nearly a year and a half on the ice. Shackleton, known as "The Boss," took five men and sailed 800 miles in an open boat from Elephant Island to the island of South Georgia, then went back and 3saved the rest of his crew, all of whom survived. Almost two years after Georgia, then went back and saved the rest of his crew, all of whom survived. Almost two years after starting out, they reached safety in South America in September 1916. In spite of his heroics, Shackleton had a hard time back in England with finances and alcohol. He 4eventually managed to get Shackleton had a hard time back in England with finances and alcohol. He eventually managed to get financing for another voyage to Antarctica in 1921, but he had a fatal heart attack at South Georgia Island and 5never made it. Island and never made it. protected preserved saved salvaged gradually eventually ultimately quickly never ever over not_ever
空格 1expedition expedition / exploration / journey / mission
語意the failed ___ of the Endurance，下文 Scott's discovery expedition 重複，指南極『遠征』
✗ exploration/journey/mission 雖近義，但全文反覆使用 expedition 指這類極地科考遠征
空格 2hope desire / hope / wish / faith
搭配in the hope of（懷著…的希望）為固定片語，後接 being the first
✗ desire/wish/faith 皆無法構成 in the hope of 這個固定片語
空格 3saved protected / preserved / saved / salvaged
語意went back and ___ the rest of his crew, all of whom survived，去救回其餘船員，用 saved『拯救』
✗ protected/preserved/salvaged 偏保護/保存/打撈（物），救人應用 saved
空格 4eventually gradually / eventually / ultimately / quickly
語意He ___ managed to get financing，歷經困難後『終於』取得資金，eventually 表結果
✗ gradually（逐漸）語意不合、ultimately 偏『最終究極』語氣略重、quickly 與『歷經困難』矛盾
空格 5never never / ever / over
語意had a fatal heart attack...and ___ made it，因心臟病發『始終沒能』抵達，用 never made it
搭配never made it（沒能成功抵達）為慣用語
✗ ever 需搭否定/疑問語境、over 為副詞/介系詞皆無法構成『never made it』
473.Enough Exercise(足夠的鍛煉)4 格#080487
One thing is certain. Most people do not get enough exercise in their 1ordinary routines. All of the advances of modern technology - from 2electric can openers to power steering - have made life easier, advances of modern technology - from electric can openers to power steering - have made life easier, more comfortable and 3much less physically 4demanding . Yet our bodies need activity, especially if they are carrying around too much fat. Satisfying this need requires a definite plan, and a commitment. are carrying around too much fat. Satisfying this need requires a definite plan, and a commitment.
空格 1ordinary ordinary / typical / common / average
語意do not get enough exercise in their ___ routines，指『日常』例行作息，ordinary routines 自然
✗ typical/common/average 雖近義，但 ordinary routines 為最自然的『日常作息』搭配
空格 2electric electronic / electric / automated / mechanical
搭配electric can openers（電動開罐器）為固定搭配，與 power steering 並列舉例電器
✗ electronic 偏電子、automated/mechanical 與 can opener 搭配不如 electric 慣用
空格 3much more / much / many / must
詞性and ___ less physically demanding，修飾比較級 less 需用 much（much less），表程度
✗ more 與 less 矛盾、many 修飾可數複數、must 為助動詞詞性不符
空格 4demanding challenging / exacting / demanding / exhausting
搭配physically demanding（體力吃重的）為固定搭配
語意科技讓生活『less physically demanding』即較不費體力，呼應後文 bodies need activity
✗ challenging/exacting/exhausting 雖近義，但與 physically 搭配最自然且固定者為 demanding
474.Wolves and Willow(狼和柳樹)4 格#080488
From the wolf's perspective, this is clearly good news. But it also had beneficial effects on the ecology of the park, according to a study published in 2004 by William Ripple and Robert Beschta from Oregon State University. In their paper in Bio Science, the two researchers showed that reintroducing the wolves was 1correlated with increased growth of willow and cottonwood in the park. Why? Because grazing animals such as elk were 2avoiding sites from which they couldn't easily escape, the scientists 3claimed . And as the woody plants and trees grew taller and thicker, beaver 4colonies expanded. related correlated connected dependent escaping avoiding avoided hiding
空格 1correlated related / correlated / connected / dependent
搭配be correlated with（與…相關）為統計研究固定搭配，後接 with increased growth
✗ related/connected 須配 to、dependent 須配 on，與後文 with 不符
空格 2avoiding escaping / avoiding / avoided / hiding
時態elk were ___ sites，過去進行式 were + V-ing，需用現在分詞 avoiding
語意麋鹿『避開』難以逃脫的地點，avoiding 語意吻合
✗ escaping 偏逃離、avoided 為過去式不構成進行式、hiding 語意（躲藏）不合避開地點
空格 3claimed asserted / claimed / insisted / declared
語意the scientists ___，科學家『聲稱/主張』此一推論，claimed 為研究報導中性用詞
✗ asserted/insisted/declared 語氣偏強硬/堅持/宣告，與陳述研究推論的中性語氣不如 claimed 自然
空格 4colonies clusters / colonies / groups / populations
搭配beaver colonies（海狸群落）為生態學固定搭配
單複數後接 expanded，beaver ___ 用複數 colonies
✗ clusters/groups/populations 雖近義，但動物聚居群體生態學慣用 colonies
475.Emperor Penguin(帝企鵝)5 格#080489
The emperor is the giant of the penguin world and the most iconic of the birds of Antarctica. Gold patches on their ears and on the top of their chest brighten 1up their black heads. Emperors and their patches on their ears and on the top of their chest brighten up their black heads. Emperors and their closest relative, the king penguin, have unique breeding cycles, with very long chick-rearing periods. The emperor penguins breed the furthest south of any penguin species, forming large colonies on the The emperor penguins breed the furthest south of any penguin species, forming large colonies on the sea-ice surrounding the Antarctic continent. They are true Antarctic birds, rarely 2seen in the subantarctic waters. So that the chicks can fledge in the late summer season, emperors breed during subantarctic waters. So that the chicks can fledge in the late summer season, emperors breed during the cold, dark winter, with temperatures as low at - 50°C and winds 3up to 200 km per hour. They trek 50–120 km (30–75 mls) over the ice to breeding colonies which may include thousands of individuals. 50–120 km (30–75 mls) over the ice to breeding colonies which may include thousands of individuals. The female lays a single egg in May then passes it over to her mate to incubate 4whilst she goes to sea to feed. For nine weeks the male fasts, losing 45% of his body weight. The male balances the egg on to feed. For nine weeks the male fasts, losing 45% of his body weight. The male balances the egg on his feet, which are 5covered in a thick roll of skin and feathers. The egg can be 70°C warmer than the up to on upon encountered heard toughed seen down up about around
空格 1up up / to / on / upon
搭配片語動詞 brighten up（使…明亮起來），brighten up their black heads 語意完整
✗ to/on/upon 皆無法與 brighten 構成『使明亮』的片語動詞
空格 2seen encountered / heard / toughed / seen
時態rarely ___ in the subantarctic waters，被動『很少被看見』，用過去分詞 seen
語意true Antarctic birds 很少『現身』於亞南極，seen 語意吻合
✗ encountered/heard 語意偏遇到/聽到、toughed 為錯字/語意不通，皆不及 seen
空格 3up down / up / about / around
搭配winds up to 200 km per hour，up to（高達）為固定片語表上限
✗ down/about/around 皆無法構成 up to（高達…）表數值上限
空格 4whilst whilst / nonetheless / nevertheless / however
語意passes it to her mate to incubate ___ she goes to sea，表『當公企鵝孵蛋的同時母企鵝出海』的同時對比，用 whilst
詞性需連接詞引導子句 she goes to sea，whilst 為連接詞
✗ nonetheless/nevertheless/however 皆為副詞，不能引導 she goes to sea 這個子句
空格 5covered hidden / covered / put / sheltered
搭配be covered in（覆蓋著），which are covered in a thick roll of skin 為固定搭配
時態are 後接過去分詞構成被動，covered 為過去分詞
✗ hidden/sheltered 語意偏隱藏/遮蔽、put 不接 in 構成此義，皆不及 covered in

Education for Global Leadership: The Importance of International Studies and Foreign Language Education for U·S· Economic and National Security Committee for Economic Development. To 1confront Education for U·S· Economic and National Security Committee for Economic Development. To confront the twenty-first century challenges to our economy and national security, our education system must be 2strengthened to increase the foreign language skills and cultural awareness of our students. be strengthened to increase the foreign language skills and cultural awareness of our students. America's continued global leadership will depend on our students' abilities to 3interact with the world community both inside and outside our borders. community both inside and outside our borders. communicate interact interaction cooperate
空格 1confront encounter / reply / confront / response
搭配confront challenges（迎戰挑戰）為固定搭配，To confront the...challenges 語意完整
詞性To ___ 需原形動詞，confront 為動詞；response 為名詞詞性不符
✗ encounter 偏偶遇、reply 不接 challenges、response 為名詞無法接在 To 後當動詞
空格 2strengthened strengthen / strengthened
時態education system must be ___，must be 後接過去分詞構成被動，用 strengthened
✗ strengthen 為原形，無法接在 be 後構成被動語態
空格 3interact communicate / interact / interaction / cooperate
搭配interact with（與…互動），abilities to interact with the world community 語意完整
詞性to ___ with，需動詞原形，interact 為動詞；interaction 為名詞詞性不符
✗ communicate 偏溝通且常配 with 但下文 interact 較貼『互動』、cooperate 偏合作、interaction 為名詞不能接 to
477.Questionnaire(問卷調查)6 格#080491
The How I Feel About My School questionnaire, designed by experts at the University of Exeter Medical School, is 1available to download for free. It 2uses emotion-style faces with options of happy, ok or sad. It asks children to rate how they feel in seven situations including on the way to school, in the classroom and in the playground. It is 3designed to help teachers and others to 4communicate with very young children on complex emotions. The project was supported by the National Institute for Health Research Collaboration for Applied Health Research and Care South West Peninsula ( NIHR PenCLAHRC). Professor Tamsin Ford, Professor of Child and Adolescent Psychiatry at the University of Exeter Medical School, 5led the design, involving children to give feedback on which style of questionnaire they could relate to best. She said: "When we're 6carrying out research in schools, it can be really hard to meaningfully assess how very young children are feeling. We couldn't find anything that could provide what we needed, so we decided to create something." possible capable open available
空格 1available possible / capable / open / available
搭配be available to download（可供下載）為固定搭配，is available to download for free 語意完整

空格 2uses works / makes / uses
語意It ___ emotion-style faces with options，問卷『使用』表情圖示，uses 語意吻合
時態主詞 It 第三人稱單數現在式，用 uses
✗ works 不及物且語意不合、makes 與 emotion-style faces 搭配不通
空格 3designed designed / designing / planned / planning
時態It is ___ to help teachers，被動『被設計用來』，用過去分詞 designed
搭配be designed to（被設計用以）為固定句型
✗ designing 為主動分詞、planned/planning 語意偏規劃，與 be ___ to help 句型不及 designed
空格 4communicate share / deliver / communicate / relate
搭配communicate with（與…溝通），communicate with very young children 語意完整
✗ share/deliver 須接受詞且不配 with、relate 偏『產生共鳴』語意不及 communicate 直接
空格 5led lead / leading / led
時態Professor...___ the design，敘述過去事蹟用過去式 led（lead 的過去式）
✗ lead 為原形時態不符、leading 為分詞無法當此句主要動詞

搭配片語動詞 carry out research（進行研究），When we're ___ out research 語意完整
時態we're ___ out，現在進行式 be + V-ing，用 carrying
✗ performing/making/managing 皆無法與 out 構成 carry out research 這個固定片語動詞

There are a number of excellent tools available to 2practitioners for individual's cognitive health status. There are a number of excellent tools available to practitioners for basic screening and tracking of cognitive health. The Alzheimer's Association website people, but some are meant for use with younger people as well. This assessment uses patient history, observations by clinicians, and the assessment of cognition. This assessment uses patient history, observations by clinicians, and concerns 3raised by the patient, family, or caregivers. The toolkit further includes three measures 4validated for use by professionals to assess and track a patient's cognitive health. These measures validated for use by professionals to assess and track a patient's cognitive health. 空格 4validated validating / validate / validated

✗ validating 為主動分詞語意不合、validate 為原形、to_validated 不合語法
479.Zebra Stripes(斑馬條紋)4 格#080494
How did the zebra get its stripes? One theory holds that stripes help confuse predators. But stripes might be 1primarily to protect zebras from ferocious…insects. That's 2according to a study in the Journal of Experimental Biology. Horseflies are abundant in Africa. They deliver painful bites that spread of Experimental Biology. Horseflies are abundant in Africa. They deliver painful bites that spread disease and distract animals from grazing. The flies use polarized light hitting water as a guide to places to mate and lay eggs. And they read such light hitting dark mammal hides as a sign of a blood places to mate and lay eggs. And they read such light hitting dark mammal hides as a sign of a blood meal. Zebra embryos start out dark and develop their white stripes before birth. Could the stripes confuse flies? The researchers went to a farm infested with horseflies, where they set up models of confuse flies? The researchers went to a farm infested with horseflies, where they set up models of black and white stripes of varying angles and widths, thus changing the 3direction of the reflected polarized light. They tracked how the patterns affected the flies' interest. They then tested models of polarized light. They tracked how the patterns affected the flies' interest. They then tested models of horses colored black, brown, white or zebra-striped. And again tracked the effect on flies. Turns out that the black and white stripes on a zebra are optimal for 4avoiding a horsefly's attention. Meaning that the black and white stripes on a zebra are optimal for avoiding a horsefly's attention. Meaning that zebras may have evolved stripes to ward off disease-carrying insects, and to dine in peace.
空格 1primarily customarily / normally / primarily / basically

空格 2according along / similar / according / related

空格 3direction shade / lightness / direction / tune

Manufacturers can provide better products at lower costs by 2using statistical control tools, 3such as Manufacturers can provide better products at lower costs by using statistical control tools, such as control charts. Some have issues with Freud's theory being focused more on male psychosexual development, 2offering very little insight into females. Nonetheless Nevertheless Although However

詞性句首 + 逗號的轉折副詞，However 引導對比句

空格 2offering convincing / presenting / offering / proposing

✗ convincing 語意不合、presenting/proposing 與 little insight 搭配不及 offer insight 自然

語意承接前文批評理論偏重男性、視同性戀為偏差，總結為『片面』
✗ favorable/impartial/fair（有利/公正/公平）語意與批評語氣相反；sided 為複合詞後半，主填格取 one
482.Rodents(齧齒動物)6 格#080497
Rodents are the workhorses of biomedical labs. So it's important to know if they're 1stressed out, which could affect results. Now we know that lab rodents may be regularly exposed to a big stressor: men. could affect results. Now we know that lab rodents may be regularly exposed to a big stressor: men. Researchers noticed that mice showed a lower pain 2response —a sign of stress—when a human was present. So they put mice and rats into clear cubicles, where their faces were visible, and 3injected them present. So they put mice and rats into clear cubicles, where their faces were visible, and injected them with an irritant. The rodents expressed pain through grimacing when no humans were nearby. But when confronted with a male researcher, or even just his 4odoriferous T-shirt, the animals grimaced when confronted with a male researcher, or even just his odoriferous T-shirt, the animals grimaced less. Female observers did not get the same rodent 5reaction . Turns out that smelling a human male made rodents produce higher levels of a stress hormone and increase their body 6temperature . That made rodents produce higher levels of a stress hormone and increase their body temperature . That stress response also blunted their sensitivity to pain. The study is in the journal Nature Methods. Researchers may have to come up with ways to ward off the stress effects of male researchers. Because Researchers may have to come up with ways to ward off the stress effects of male researchers. Because to a rodent, men smell like hell.
空格 1stressed strained / stressing / stressed / pressured
搭配stressed out（壓力大的）為固定片語，if they're stressed out 語意完整
時態they're ___ out，be + 過去分詞作形容詞，用 stressed
✗ strained/pressured 不接 out 構成此片語、stressing 為主動分詞語意不合

時態put mice...and ___ them with an irritant，與 put 並列的過去式動詞，用 injected

語意his ___ T-shirt 指『有氣味的』T恤（散發男性氣味造成壓力），odoriferous 語意吻合，下文 smell 呼應

Intrigued by this observation, 3scientists from the University of that he'd suffered from atherosclerosis. Intrigued by this observation, scientists from the University of California, Irvine, got to wondering whether damaged arteries were common among ancient Egyptians. So they collected 20 mummies from the museum basement and from various displays, and 4subjected So they collected 20 mummies from the museum basement and from various displays, and subjected them to whole-body CT scans to look at their insides. And nine of them showed hardening of those arteries. Although no one knows what those mummies were eating, it's safe to say they never 5encountered a corn dog. 484.Big Buildings(大型建築)5 格#080499
The desire to build big is nothing new. Big buildings have been used to show off power and wealth; to honor leaders or religious beliefs; to stretch the limits of what's possible; and even as simple honor leaders or religious beliefs; to stretch the limits of what's possible; and even as simple 1competition among owners, families, architects, and builders. Some of the most 2dramatic buildings of the past include the pyramids in Egypt, the skinny towers stretching towards the sky in Italian hill the past include the pyramids in Egypt, the skinny towers stretching towards the sky in Italian hill towns, and the gothic cathedrals of France. While these types of buildings may look very different from each other, they all have one thing in 3common . They were built with masonry or stone walls each other, they all have one thing in common . They were built with masonry or stone walls supporting most of the weight (so-called load-bearing walls), including that of the floors, the people, and everything the rooms contained. Because of this, the height of these buildings was limited by how and everything the rooms contained. Because of this, the height of these buildings was limited by how massive and heavy they had to be at the base. Two 4developments in the 19th century paved the way for a whole new type of building: the skyscraper. The first was the development of a safe elevator. for a whole new type of building: the skyscraper. The first was the development of a safe elevator. Primitive elevators of various designs had been used for centuries, and starting in the mid 19th century, steam-operated elevators were used to move materials in factories, mines, and warehouses. century, steam-operated elevators were used to move materials in factories, mines, and warehouses. But these elevators were not 5considered safe for people; if the cable broke, they would plummet to the bottom of the elevator shaft. bottom of the elevator shaft. interesting weird dramatic strange usual common difference similarity improvements technologies advantages developments

✗ interesting/weird/strange（有趣/怪異/奇怪）皆不及 dramatic 表達宏偉震撼的正面語氣

搭配have one thing in common（有一個共同點）為固定片語

485.Below-ground Organisms(地下生物)4 格#080500
"If you want to find unique 1diversity and if you want to find a wide range of 2different below-ground organisms, you don't have to travel around the world. You can walk across Central Park." That organisms, you don't have to travel around the world. You can walk across Central Park." That statement comes from Noah Fierer, an ecology and evolutionary biology professor at the University of Colorado, Boulder. He's also a coauthor of a study that uncovered the surprisingly large diversity of Colorado, Boulder. He's also a coauthor of a study that uncovered the surprisingly large diversity of subterranean microbial life at the 843-acre green rectangle in the heart of Manhattan. The research is in the Proceedings of the Royal Society B. Investigators looked at 596 3separate soil samples from the in the Proceedings of the Royal Society B. Investigators looked at 596 separate soil samples from the park and found thousands of different types of 4microbes . They also compared those microbes with those living in 52 other soil samples taken from all around the planet. The park had organisms that those living in 52 other soil samples taken from all around the planet. The park had organisms that also exist in deserts, frozen tundra, forests, rainforests and prairies. Antarctica was the only area that had microbes that did not overlap with those found in Central Park. And only a small percentage of the had microbes that did not overlap with those found in Central Park. And only a small percentage of the park's microbes were found to be already listed in databases. The variety of microbes probably reflects a diversity of soil conditions within the park. Seems that New York is a melting pot for people above a diversity of soil conditions within the park. Seems that New York is a melting pot for people above the surface. And for microbes beneath it.
空格 1diversity difference / diversity / collection / unity
語意下文 "a wide range of different below-ground organisms" 與後段 "surprisingly large diversity" 都在講「多樣性」，呼應 diversity。
搭配unique 常與 diversity 搭配，指獨特的多樣性。
✗ difference 指個別差異、collection 是收集、unity 是統一，都不符「多樣性」語意。
空格 2different similar / different / divergent / differential
搭配"a wide range of ___ organisms" 慣用 different，表「各式各樣不同的」。
詞性需形容詞修飾 organisms，different 為基本形容詞最自然。
✗ similar 語意相反；divergent、differential 雖近義但語域與搭配不合此口語句。
空格 3separate separate / separated / spare / respective
詞性修飾 "596 ___ soil samples"，需形容詞 separate（個別的）。
搭配separate samples 為固定搭配，指「分別採集的樣本」。
✗ separated 為過去分詞強調被分開的動作、spare 是多餘的、respective 指各自的需有對應對象，皆不合。
空格 4microbes microscopes / organisms / microbes / lives
語意全文主題為 "microbial life"、後句即用 "those microbes" 回指，故此處應為 microbes。
單複數"thousands of different types of ___" 需複數可數名詞。
✗ microscopes 是顯微鏡（工具）、organisms 較廣泛但下文用 microbes 回指、lives 不合語境。
486.Star Watching(觀星)5 格#080501
Break out the binoculars and telescopes on Saturday, May 19th. Because it's the first International Break out the binoculars and telescopes on Saturday, May 19th. Because it's the first International Sidewalk Astronomy Night. "Sidewalk Astronomers" was founded in San Francisco in 1968, when a lot of people were seeing stars, and not just at night. The group's goal is to 1expose more people to the of people were seeing stars, and not just at night. The group's goal is to expose more people to the beauty and wonder of celestial objects with good viewing equipment and to provide 2information about what the eyewitnesses are actually looking at. The most 3frequently viewed objects are the moon; about what the eyewitnesses are actually looking at. The most frequently viewed objects are the moon; Jupiter, whose major moons are visible with good binocs; and Saturn. I once trained a cheap telescope on Saturn and some friends were so amazed at the site of the rings they seriously looked at the other on Saturn and some friends were so amazed at the site of the rings they seriously looked at the other end of the scope to see if I had 4doctored it. Sidewalk Astronomy Night is indeed an international event, with dozens of public viewing sites from Beijing to the corner of 81st Street and Central Park event, with dozens of public viewing sites from Beijing to the corner of 81st Street and Central Park West in Manhattan. For more info and to find a site near you, just go to the official website. And if there's no 5organized site near you, feel free to go outside and look at the stars anyway. there's no organized site near you, feel free to go outside and look at the stars anyway. evidence information data report necessarily importantly frequently regularly tailored doctored fixed fitted
空格 1expose disclose / circulate / expose / encourage
搭配固定搭配 "expose someone to something"，下文接 "to the beauty and wonder"。
語意宗旨是讓更多人「接觸／見識」天體之美。
✗ disclose 是揭露秘密、circulate 是流通、encourage 後不接 "to + 名詞" 此結構，皆不合。
空格 2information evidence / information / data / report
搭配"provide information about" 為固定搭配，下文接 about what they are looking at。
語意提供「資訊」說明觀看對象，最貼切。
✗ evidence 是證據、data 偏數據、report 是報告（可數需冠詞），均不如 information 通順。
空格 3frequently necessarily / importantly / frequently / regularly
搭配"the most ___ viewed objects" 慣用 frequently（最常被觀看的）。
語意語意指「最常被觀測」的天體：月亮、木星、土星。
✗ necessarily 是必然地、importantly 是重要地、regularly 偏規律性，都不符「最常」的頻率最高級語意。
空格 4doctored tailored / doctored / fixed / fitted
語意朋友懷疑望遠鏡被「動過手腳／造假」才看到土星環，doctored 指竄改造假。
時態"if I had ___ it" 過去完成式需過去分詞。
✗ tailored 是量身訂製、fixed 是修理／固定、fitted 是裝配，皆無「造假動手腳」之意。
空格 5organized selected / situated / edited / organized
搭配"organized site" 指「有規劃舉辦的觀星地點」，與下文 "no organized site near you" 呼應一致。
詞性需過去分詞當形容詞修飾 site。
✗ selected、situated、edited 都不符「主辦／籌辦的活動場地」之意。
487.Amount of Sleep(睡眠量)5 格#080502
The amount of sleep you need depends on many 1factors , especially your age. Newborns sleep between 16 and 18 hours a day and preschool children should sleep between 10 and 12 hours. Older between 16 and 18 hours a day and preschool children should sleep between 10 and 12 hours. Older children and teens need at least nine hours to be well-rested. For most adults, seven to eight hours a night appears to the best amount of sleep. However, for some people" enough sleep" may be as few night appears to the best amount of sleep. However, for some people" enough sleep" may be as few as five hours or as many as 10 hours of sleep. As you get older, your sleeping 2patterns change. Older adults tend to sleep more lightly and awaken more frequently in the night than younger adults. This adults tend to sleep more lightly and awaken more frequently in the night than younger adults. This can have many causes including medical conditions and medications used to treat them. But there’s no evidence that older adults need less sleep than younger adults. Getting enough sleep is 3important no evidence that older adults need less sleep than younger adults. Getting enough sleep is important to your health because it boosts your 4immune system, which makes your body better able to fight disease. Sleep is necessary for your nervous system to work properly. Too little sleep makes you disease. Sleep is necessary for your nervous system to work properly. Too little sleep makes you drowsy and unable to concentrate. It also impairs memory and physical performance. So how many hours of sleep are enough for You? Experts say that if you feel drowsy during the day — even during hours of sleep are enough for You? Experts say that if you feel drowsy during the day — even during boring activities - you are not getting enough sleep. Also, the quality of sleep is just as important as quantity. People whose sleep is frequently interrupted or cut short are not getting quality sleep. If you quantity. People whose sleep is frequently interrupted or cut short are not getting quality sleep. If you experience frequent daytime sleepiness, even after increasing the amount of quality sleep you get, talk to your doctor. He or she may be able to 5identify the cause of sleep problems and offer advice on how to your doctor. He or she may be able to identify the cause of sleep problems and offer advice on how to get a better night’s sleep.
空格 1factors factors / facts / situations / fields
語意"depends on many ___, especially your age"，age 是其中一個「因素」，故為 factors。
單複數many 後需複數可數名詞。
✗ facts 是事實、situations 是情況、fields 是領域，皆不符 "depends on" 的「因素」語意。
空格 2patterns orders / patterns / standards / systems
搭配"sleeping patterns" 為固定搭配（睡眠模式），下文也再次出現此概念。
語意講年齡漸長睡眠型態改變，patterns 最貼切。
✗ orders 是順序、standards 是標準、systems 是系統，搭配 sleeping 都不自然。
空格 3important healthy / functional / important / insignificant
語意"Getting enough sleep is ___ to your health because it boosts your immune system"，因果說明睡眠對健康「重要」。
搭配"important to your health" 為常見搭配。
✗ healthy 修飾對象錯、functional 是功能性的、insignificant 語意相反。
空格 4immune body / mental / immune / exempted
搭配"immune system" 免疫系統為固定搭配，下文 "fight disease" 呼應。
詞性需形容詞修飾 system。
✗ body system 太籠統、mental system 不是固定搭配、exempted 不通。
空格 5identify research / announce / identify / demonstrate
搭配"identify the cause of" 為固定搭配（找出原因）。
語意醫生「找出／辨識」睡眠問題的原因並給建議。
✗ research 偏研究、announce 是宣布、demonstrate 是示範證明，皆不符「找出原因」。
488.Capsaicin(辣椒素)5 格#080503
Here's a possible blood 1pressure remedy. But it's only for those who can stand the heat. It's capsaicin, Here's a possible blood pressure remedy. But it's only for those who can stand the heat. It's capsaicin, the "active ingredient" in peppers like habaneros that should probably be sold by prescription only. While lips burn and eyes water, blood vessels 2actually relax, thanks to increased 3production of the While lips burn and eyes water, blood vessels actually relax, thanks to increased production of the signaling molecule nitric oxide. In rodents, anyway. For seven months, researchers in China fed a steady diet of capsaicin to rats bred to be hypertensive. Long-term consumption of the chemical steady diet of capsaicin to rats bred to be hypertensive. Long-term consumption of the chemical 4substantially lowered the rats' blood pressure. The results appear in the August issue of the journal Cell Metabolism. Previous research found mixed results with capsaicin, but those studies only looked at Metabolism. Previous research found mixed results with capsaicin, but those studies only looked at 5short-term effects. Human trials are needed, but there's already a clue. Some 20 percent of people in northeastern China have high blood pressure. But the southwest—where hot peppers are a dietary northeastern China have high blood pressure. But the southwest—where hot peppers are a dietary staple—has a much lower incidence, half in some places. Human studies could also confirm whether the habanero, as legend has it, can cause hearing loss. Allegedly so that diners don't have to listen to the habanero, as legend has it, can cause hearing loss. Allegedly so that diners don't have to listen to
空格 1pressure flow / stress / circulation / pressure
搭配"blood pressure" 血壓為固定搭配，全文主題即降血壓。
語意首句 "blood pressure remedy" 已點題。
✗ flow、circulation 偏血液循環、stress 是壓力但非醫學上的血壓詞，皆不符 "blood ___" 此固定詞。
空格 2actually thankfully / actually / really / undoubtedly
語意形成轉折：嘴唇灼燒眼睛流淚，血管「其實／反而」放鬆，actually 帶出意外對比。
詞性需副詞修飾動詞 relax。
✗ thankfully 帶感激語氣、really 偏強調、undoubtedly 是無疑地，皆不如 actually 表「出乎意料」貼切。
空格 3production creation / reaction / production / publication
搭配"production of nitric oxide" 指一氧化氮的「生成」，為固定搭配。
語意血管放鬆是因信號分子 NO 的「增加生成」。
✗ creation 偏抽象創造、reaction 是反應、publication 是出版，皆不符生理「生成量」語意。
空格 4substantially substantially / abnormally / slightly / instinctively
語意長期攝取辣椒素「大幅」降低血壓，與下文「半數」效果一致，substantially 表幅度大。
詞性需副詞修飾動詞 lowered。
✗ abnormally 是異常地、slightly 是輕微地（與結論相反）、instinctively 是本能地，皆不合。
空格 5short short / term / momentary / fleeting
搭配"short-term effects" 短期效應為固定搭配，與前文 "Long-term consumption" 形成對比。
語意先前研究結果不一，因為只看「短期」效應。
✗ term 本身是名詞無法構成 "___-term"、momentary／fleeting 雖近義但不與 -term 搭配成固定詞。
489.Social Influence(社會影響力)6 格#080504
If you want to guess how many jelly beans are in a jar, you should ask your friends. Then average their If you want to guess how many jelly beans are in a jar, you should ask your friends. Then average their answers. Because a group guess is often more accurate than that of any one individual. Just don't let them peek at each other's responses. Because a new study shows that social influence can sway them peek at each other's responses. Because a new study shows that social influence can sway people's 1estimate and render the crowd 2incorrect . The work appears in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences . Crowd wisdom is actually a statistical phenomenon. Gather enough National Academy of Sciences . Crowd wisdom is actually a statistical phenomenon. Gather enough estimates and the wild guesses cancel each other out, bringing you closer to the answer. But psychology and statistics don't 3mix . And knowing what your peers think doesn't make you any psychology and statistics don't mix . And knowing what your peers think doesn't make you any smarter. European scientists asked volunteers to estimate statistics like the population density of Switzerland. Each person got five guesses. Some were shown their peers' answers and others weren't. Switzerland. Each person got five guesses. Some were shown their peers' answers and others weren't. Turns out that seeing others' estimates led to a lot of second guessing, which 4narrowed the range of the group's responses and pointed them in the wrong 5direction . Even 6worse , knowing that others said the group's responses and pointed them in the wrong direction . Even worse , knowing that others said the same thing made everyone more confident they were right. So there is wisdom in numbers—as long as those numbers keep quiet `til they're counted. long as those numbers keep quiet `til they're counted. match agree mix coexist changed swayed narrowed influenced place degree situation direction
空格 1estimate evaluation / assess / estimate / expectation
語意社會影響力會左右人們的「估計值」，下文反覆出現 estimate(s)。
詞性"sway people's ___" 需名詞作受詞，estimate 為名詞。
✗ assess 是動詞（詞性錯）、evaluation 偏正式評估、expectation 是期望，皆不符 "crowd 估計值"。
空格 2incorrect indifferent / hesitant / right / incorrect
語意社會影響力會左右估計並使群眾「不正確」，與後文 "wrong direction" 一致。
搭配"render + 受詞 + 形容詞"，需形容詞 incorrect。
✗ right 語意相反、indifferent 是冷漠、hesitant 是猶豫，皆不符「使答案錯誤」。
空格 3mix match / agree / mix / coexist
語意"psychology and statistics don't ___"，意指兩者「不相容」，下文又重複 "don't mix"。
時態助動詞 don't 後接原形動詞 mix。
✗ match、agree、coexist 雖近義但 "don't mix" 才與後文重複句完全一致。
空格 4narrowed changed / swayed / narrowed / influenced
語意看到別人的估計造成二次猜測，「縮小」了群體答案的範圍 (range)，narrow the range 為自然搭配。
搭配"narrowed the range" 固定搭配。
✗ changed 太籠統、swayed 是左右、influenced 是影響，皆不如 narrowed 精準對應 "range"。
空格 5direction place / degree / situation / direction
搭配"pointed them in the wrong ___"，固定片語 "in the wrong direction"。
語意指向「錯誤方向」。
✗ place、degree、situation 都無法與 "point in the wrong ___" 構成固定片語。
空格 6worse better / more / though / worse
搭配"Even ___," 句首轉折慣用 "Even worse"（更糟的是）。
語意下文說自信反而更高，是「更糟」的後果。
✗ better 語意相反、more／though 無法與 Even 構成 "Even ___," 句首慣用語。
490.Nepalese Gharial(尼泊爾加裏亞爾)5 格#080505
Which would you rather see: a rare Nepalese gharial or a common vole? Even without knowing what these animals are, you might be more intrigued by the gharial, simply because it is rare. This is the these animals are, you might be more intrigued by the gharial, simply because it is rare. This is the conclusion of research 1published in the journal Public Library of Science ONE. The researchers say that labeling endangered animals as "rare," may harm the species, raising its value and so putting it at labeling endangered animals as "rare," may harm the species, raising its value and so putting it at greater risk of being exploited within the illegal 2pet trade , for either medicines or exotic zoos. Scientists created a Web site where users could view images of either "rare" or "common" animals. Scientists created a Web site where users could view images of either "rare" or "common" animals. When users clicked on one of the two options, up popped a progress bar showing the images in the process of downloading. But, 3irritatingly , the download took a 4whopping six minutes . When the process of downloading. But, irritatingly , the download took a whopping six minutes . When the download finally completed, the images were designed not to show up! Well, significantly more users chose the rare 5slideshow , waited for it to download and often tried again after it didn't work. So, chose the rare slideshow , waited for it to download and often tried again after it didn't work. So, maybe to save the endangered gharial, we ought to just call it just a tired, ugly crocodile. 2 pet_exchange pet_sale pet_export pet_trade
空格 1published published / publish / publishing / publishes
時態過去分詞 published 作後位修飾 research（被發表的研究）。
詞性the conclusion of research [published in...]，分詞片語修飾名詞。
✗ publish/publishes/publishing 均無法構成「被動後位修飾」的分詞片語。
空格 2pet trade pet exchange / pet sale / pet export / pet trade
搭配「illegal pet trade」是固定說法（非法寵物交易）。
語意後文接 medicines、exotic zoos，指整體交易市場 trade。
✗ pet exchange/sale/export 都非此語境慣用語，trade 才是非法買賣的標準用詞。
空格 3irritatingly shockingly / irritatingly / pleasantly / irrelevantly
語意下載要等六分鐘令人惱火，irritatingly（令人惱怒地）符合語氣。
✗ shockingly/pleasantly 情緒方向不合；irrelevantly（不相關地）語意不通。
空格 4whopping quickly / whopping / massively / potentially
搭配「a whopping six minutes」是常見強調說法（多達整整六分鐘）。
詞性形容詞修飾 six minutes，whopping 表「龐大的」。
✗ quickly/massively 為副詞不能直接修飾名詞片語；potentially 語意不合。
空格 5slideshow figure / icon / slideshow / pause
語意前文 view images、download，使用者選的是「圖片幻燈片」slideshow。
✗ figure/icon/pause 都無法對應「下載圖片組」的語境。
491.Bacteria Sinus Infections(細菌性鼻窦感染)5 格#080506
This is the season when 1bacterial sinus infections run rampant. But some people are better able to This is the season when bacterial sinus infections run rampant. But some people are better able to ward off that malady. And they can be identified by their taste buds. Because those of us who can better detect bitter flavors may also be better equipped to fight off upper 2respiratory tract infections. better detect bitter flavors may also be better equipped to fight off upper respiratory tract infections. The finding is in the Journal of Clinical Investigation. It turns out that those bitter taste 3receptors aren't just in our mouths. They're also in our noses. Being better able to sense bitterness may be a first line of just in our mouths. They're also in our noses. Being better able to sense bitterness may be a first line of defense against bacteria. That's because those bacteria 4actually taste bitter to us. So when taste receptors in our airways sense a bitter flavor they roll out the 5defenses . Cilia lining our sinuses beat receptors in our airways sense a bitter flavor they roll out the defenses . Cilia lining our sinuses beat faster, to sweep away germs. And bacteria-killing nitric oxide gets released into our nasal cavities. Better bitter sensing means a bigger response. It's one reason researchers at the Monell Chemical Better bitter sensing means a bigger response. It's one reason researchers at the Monell Chemical Senses Center are trying to come up with a universal taste test—a standardized measurement of how well we sense flavors. Because one key to living healthier could be on the tip of your tongue. well we sense flavors. Because one key to living healthier could be on the tip of your tongue. breathing respiratory nosy nose signals receptors members accessories simply only actually basically
空格 1bacterial medical / bacterium / bacterial / popular
詞性修飾名詞 "sinus infections" 需形容詞 bacterial（細菌性的）。
語意標題與全文講 "bacterial sinus infections" 細菌性鼻竇感染。
✗ bacterium 是名詞（單數菌）、medical 太籠統、popular 是受歡迎的，皆不符。
空格 2respiratory breathing / respiratory / nosy / nose
搭配"upper respiratory tract infections" 上呼吸道感染為固定醫學搭配。
詞性需形容詞修飾 "tract"。
✗ breathing 是動名詞、nosy 是愛管閒事的、nose 是名詞，皆不能修飾 "tract"。
空格 3receptors signals / receptors / members / accessories
搭配"bitter taste receptors" 苦味受體為固定搭配，下文 "taste receptors" 重複出現。
單複數"those bitter taste ___ aren't just in our mouths" 需複數名詞。
✗ signals 是信號、members 是成員、accessories 是配件，皆不符生理「受體」。
空格 4actually simply / only / actually / basically
語意"those bacteria ___ taste bitter to us"，actually 帶出「其實」的意外事實。
詞性需副詞修飾動詞 taste。
✗ simply 是僅僅、only 是只、basically 是基本上，皆不如 actually 表「事實上竟然」貼切。
空格 5defenses fights / defenses / acts / equipment
搭配"roll out the defenses" 展開防禦，與前句 "first line of defense" 呼應。
單複數"roll out the ___" 此處用複數 defenses 指各種防禦機制。
✗ fights、acts、equipment 皆不符「啟動防禦機制」之意，且 equipment 不可數。
492.Society and Culture(社會與文化)4 格#080507
For too long we have held preconceived notions of ‘the’ market and ‘the’ state that were seemingly independent of local societies and cultures. The debate about civil society ultimately is seemingly independent of local societies and cultures. The debate about civil society ultimately is about how culture, market and state 1relate to each other. Concern about civil society, however, is not only relevant to central and eastern Europe and the developing world. It is very much of 2interest to the only relevant to central and eastern Europe and the developing world. It is very much of interest to the European Union as well. The Civil Dialogue Initiated by the Commission in the 1990s was a first attempt by the EU to give the institutions of society - and not only governments and businesses-a attempt by the EU to give the institutions of society - and not only governments and businesses-a voice at the policy-making tables in Brussels. The EU, like other international institutions, has a long way to go in trying to 3accommodate the frequently divergent interests of non-governmental way to go in trying to accommodate the frequently divergent interests of non-governmental organizations and citizen groups. There is increasing 4recognition that international and national governments have to open up to civil society institutions. governments have to open up to civil society institutions. 1 fight_against respond_to agree_with relate_to interest_in interested_to interested_in interest_to 2 interest_in interested_to interested_in interest_to apply accommodate tolerate connect recognition rectification intention debate
空格 1relate to fight against / respond to / agree with / relate to
搭配「how culture, market and state relate to each other」搭配 relate to each other（彼此關聯）。
語意全句談三者如何相互關聯，relate to 最貼切。
✗ fight against/respond to/agree with 與 each other 搭配後語意都偏離「相互關聯」。
空格 2interest to interest in / interested to / interested in / interest to
搭配「of interest to the EU」是 of interest to sb（對某人有意義）固定片語。
詞性of 後接名詞 interest，再接 to＋對象，故為 interest to。
✗ interested in/to 為形容詞，不能接在 of 後；interest in 介系詞錯（此處要 to）。
空格 3accommodate apply / accommodate / tolerate / connect
搭配「accommodate the divergent interests」搭配（容納／顧及分歧的利益）。
語意EU 要設法「兼容」非政府組織分歧的利益。
✗ apply/connect 不接 interests 此語意；tolerate（容忍）語氣偏負面、不貼切。
空格 4recognition recognition / rectification / intention / debate
搭配「increasing recognition that...」是常見搭配（越來越多人認知到…）。
語意後接 that 同位語子句說明被認知的內容。
✗ rectification/intention/debate 與 increasing...that 子句語意搭配都不通。
493.Sports Wins Influence Elections(體育勝利影響選舉)5 格#080508
When it comes to 1elections , sometimes we vote with our heads and sometimes with our hearts. But scientists at Stanford say we might also be voting with our pompoms. Because they've found that our scientists at Stanford say we might also be voting with our pompoms. Because they've found that our behavior at the polls is influenced by the results of local 2sporting events, work published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. Humans are emotional 3creatures . And our strong Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences. Humans are emotional creatures . And our strong feelings about one thing can spread to another. So the Stanford scientists wondered whether events that are unrelated to government performance might sway the way people feel about their elected that are unrelated to government performance might sway the way people feel about their elected officials. And what could be less 4relevant to the workings in Washington or your state capital than college football? The researchers looked at the election results from 20 years' worth of 5presidential , college football? The researchers looked at the election results from 20 years' worth of presidential , senatorial and gubernatorial races. And they found that a home-team win before the election gave the incumbent a boost of almost two percentage points. The more beloved the team, the bigger the incumbent a boost of almost two percentage points. The more beloved the team, the bigger the bounce. And it's not just football. In a separate survey, the scientists found that NCAA college basketball results affected presidential approval ratings. So next election day, you might think about basketball results affected presidential approval ratings. So next election day, you might think about practicing a little separation of stadium and state.
空格 1elections electoral / lectorates / elections / elects
搭配"When it comes to ___" 後接名詞，elections 與全文主題（選舉）一致。
詞性介系詞 to 後需名詞，elections 為複數名詞。
✗ electoral 是形容詞、elects 是動詞、lectorates 拼字不對，皆詞性或拼字錯誤。
空格 2sporting sporting / musical / historical / group
搭配"local sporting events" 體育賽事為固定搭配，全文討論球賽影響選舉。
詞性需形容詞修飾 events。
✗ musical、historical、group 皆不符全文「運動賽事」主題。
空格 3creatures being / creature / creatures / animals
單複數"Humans are emotional ___" 主詞 Humans 為複數，補語需複數 creatures。
搭配"emotional creatures" 情感動物為常見表達。
✗ creature 單數與 Humans 不一致、being 單數、animals 雖複數但搭配 emotional 不如 creatures 慣用。
空格 4relevant motivated / relevant / exciting / emotional
搭配"less ___ to" 後接 to，relevant to（與…相關）為固定搭配。
語意大學美式足球與華府運作「最不相關」，諷刺其無關卻能影響選情。
✗ motivated、exciting、emotional 皆無法與 "less ___ to the workings" 構成「相關性」對比。
空格 5presidential president / presidential / presidency / present
詞性與並列的 senatorial、gubernatorial 同為形容詞修飾 races，需 presidential。
搭配"presidential races" 總統選舉為固定搭配。
✗ president、presidency 是名詞、present 是現在的，詞性與語意皆不合並列形容詞結構。
494.Doctors' Messages(醫生留言)5 格#080509
The messages doctors give can 1influence some treatments' effectiveness. For example, it seems that The messages doctors give can influence some treatments' effectiveness. For example, it seems that information from a doctor affects outcomes for migraine 2sufferers — whether they got a real drug or a placebo. Researchers studied 66 people with recurring migraines over the course of seven attacks. placebo. Researchers studied 66 people with recurring migraines over the course of seven attacks. During the first 3headache , the 4patients received no treatment. For the next six, they were given either a placebo or a drug. Each time, regardless of which they got, some were told it was the drug, some a placebo or a drug. Each time, regardless of which they got, some were told it was the drug, some were told it was the placebo, and the rest were informed that it could be either the drug or the placebo. Overall, the drug did work better than the placebo. But the placebo worked, too—both when placebo. Overall, the drug did work better than the placebo. But the placebo worked, too—both when the patients were told it was a placebo, and even better when the doctors told them that they were getting the drug. That is, when the docs set their expectations high. Meanwhile, the actual drug was getting the drug. That is, when the docs set their expectations high. Meanwhile, the actual drug was least effective when the patients thought it was a placebo. The study is in the journal Science Translational Medicine. The physician's input thus plays a role in how migraine patients fare. Tell them Translational Medicine. The physician's input thus plays a role in how migraine patients fare. Tell them they're getting a good treatment, and they're 5apparently more likely to respond.
空格 1influence cause / result / contribute / influence
時態"messages doctors give can ___"，情態動詞 can 後接原形動詞 influence。
語意全文講醫生的話「影響」療效。
✗ cause、result、contribute 後需特定搭配（result in、contribute to），直接接受詞 effectiveness 不通；influence 可直接及物。
空格 2sufferers patient / suffers / sufferers / recipients
搭配"migraine sufferers" 偏頭痛患者為固定搭配。
單複數需複數名詞指一群患者。
✗ suffers 是動詞、patient 單數、recipients 偏「接受者」不如 sufferers 精準。
空格 3headache blow / attack / headache / war
語意"During the first ___, the patients received no treatment"，指第一次「頭痛發作」，與偏頭痛主題一致。
搭配下文 "the next six" 指後續發作，與 headache/attack 序列呼應。
✗ blow 是打擊、war 是戰爭、attack 雖近義但 headache 更直接對應偏頭痛語境。
空格 4patients person / patients / doctors / members
語意"when the ___ were told it was a placebo"，被告知的是「病人」，與全文受試者一致。
單複數與後文 "they were getting the drug" 複數呼應，需複數 patients。
✗ person 單數、doctors 是告知方非被告知方、members 不合語境。
空格 5apparently temporarily / eventually / basically / apparently
語意"more likely to respond" 前用 apparently（看來／顯然）表觀察到的結果。
詞性需副詞修飾形容詞片語 "more likely"。
✗ temporarily 是暫時地、eventually 是最終、basically 是基本上，皆不符「看來」的推論語氣。
495.Good Sense (理智)3 格#080511
Good sense appears to have 1prevailed at last. With a fresh set of draft rules to replace last year's poorly Good sense appears to have prevailed at last. With a fresh set of draft rules to replace last year's poorly conceived ones, the centre has sought to withdraw the ban on sale of cattle for slaughter in animal markets. The draft rules are now open for comments and suggestions. When the Union Ministry for markets. The draft rules are now open for comments and suggestions. When the Union Ministry for environment, forests and climate change notified the rules under the prevention of the cruelty to animals Act on May 23, 2017, there was 2concern that in the name of preventing cruelty to animals and animals Act on May 23, 2017, there was concern that in the name of preventing cruelty to animals and regulating livestock markets the government was surreptitiously throttling the cattle trade and furthering the BJP's cow protection agenda. The rules were criticized for restricting legitimate animal furthering the BJP's cow protection agenda. The rules were criticized for restricting legitimate animal trade and interfering with 3dietary habits.
空格 1prevailed existed / created / prevailed / come
搭配"Good sense ... has prevailed at last" 固定搭配（理智終於佔上風）。
時態"appears to have ___" 需過去分詞，prevailed 合完成式。
✗ existed、created、come 雖為分詞形但語意不符「理智終於佔上風」這個慣用表達。
空格 2concern advertisement / concern / campaign / circumstance
語意"there was ___ that ... the government was surreptitiously throttling the cattle trade"，指外界的「憂慮」。
搭配"there was concern that" 固定句型。
✗ advertisement 是廣告、campaign 是活動、circumstance 是情況，皆不符 "concern that" 的憂慮語意。
空格 3dietary healthy / vegetarian / dietary / dairy
搭配"dietary habits" 飲食習慣為固定搭配，下文批評規則干涉飲食習慣。
詞性需形容詞修飾 habits。
✗ healthy、vegetarian、dairy 雖為形容詞但語意過窄，不如 dietary 泛指「飲食」貼切。
496.Natural-born Runners (天生的跑步者)5 格#080512
You've probably had the feeling. Your running shoes are pounding the pavement — then suddenly You've probably had the feeling. Your running shoes are pounding the pavement — then suddenly your pain fades away, and you're feeling euphoric. The runner's high. But that biological perk may be limited to mammals that evolved for endurance 1exercise — like us. So says a study in the Journal of limited to mammals that evolved for endurance exercise — like us. So says a study in the Journal of Experimental Biology. Researchers had humans and dogs — both natural-born runners — jog a half hour on a treadmill. Then they sampled their blood for endocannabinoids, some of the 2compounds hour on a treadmill. Then they sampled their blood for endocannabinoids, some of the compounds thought to trigger the runner's high. As expected, humans and dogs had much higher levels after the run. But when ferrets — a 3sedentary species — took the same 30-minute trot, they had no spike in run. But when ferrets — a sedentary species — took the same 30-minute trot, they had no spike in those feel-good molecules. The authors say that's because long-distance running could have helped our hunter-gatherer ancestors find more food — thus increasing their reproductive success. And they our hunter-gatherer ancestors find more food — thus increasing their reproductive success. And they speculate that natural selection may have linked up a feel-good reward to that beneficial 5behavior .
