語意許多房屋「堵塞」狹窄街道，造成擁擠，blocked 呼應 crowded living conditions。
搭配「blocked the narrow streets」搭配（堵住街道）。
✗ ploughed/tallied/endowed 與 narrow streets 搭配及語意都不通（耕/計算/捐贈）。
空格 2the number of an amount of / the number of / a few of / a lot
搭配「the number of meals served」固定搭配（…的數量），後接可數複數。
單複數meals 為可數複數，用 the number of。
✗ an amount of 用於不可數；a few of/a lot 與 meals served 結構及語意不合。
空格 3held held / confined / refined / heralded
搭配「festivals are held」固定搭配（舉辦節慶），被動語態。
時態are＋過去分詞 held，被動表「被舉行」。
✗ confined/refined/heralded 與 festivals 搭配語意都不合（限制/精煉/預告）。
530.Roommates( 室友)4 格#080551
When living with roommates, it is important to 1share responsibility for household tasks. Although sometimes challenging, the experience is often 2worthwhile because it teaches cooperation and independence. Roommates should also 3divide bills fairly and determine the most important 4factors that make living together successful, such as respect and communication.
空格 1share share / take / form / do
搭配share responsibility 是固定搭配（分擔責任）
語意室友共同分擔 household tasks，語意是「分攤」責任
✗ take/do responsibility 不是標準搭配（應為 take responsibility for 但語意偏「承擔全部」），form responsibility 不通
空格 2worthwhile worth / worthwhile / worthy / worthing
詞性be 動詞 is 後接形容詞當主詞補語，worthwhile 為形容詞「值得的」
搭配the experience is worthwhile 是自然搭配（經驗很值得）
✗ worth 需接受詞(worth doing)、worthy 多接 of、worthing 非正確字
空格 3divide divide / conclude / collect / commit
搭配divide bills 是搭配（分攤帳單），呼應 fairly 公平分配
語意室友把帳單「平分」，語意需要分配的動詞
✗ conclude(總結)、collect(收集)、commit(承諾)語意都不符「公平分攤帳單」
空格 4factors ends / causes / characteristics / factors
搭配the most important factors 是常見搭配（最重要的因素）
語意後接 such as respect and communication，這些是促成成功的「因素 factors」
✗ ends(目的)、causes(原因)、characteristics(特徵)語意較弱；句意指決定成功的要素，factors 最貼切
531.Video Game(視頻遊戲)3 格#080552
IBM arranged a video qame match between a supercomputer and human qamers.The fact that the IBM arranged a video qame match between a supercomputer and human qamers.The fact that the computer has 1competed against humans made people worry whether scientific technologies could threaten us. 2However , the computer can only perform programs that are set by us. Even machine- threaten us. However , the computer can only perform programs that are set by us. Even machine- learning has not 3yet developed enough to think independently like humans.
空格 1competed competed / fought / challenged / acted
搭配compete against humans 是固定搭配（與人類競賽），呼應 a match between
語意電腦在 video game match 中與人類「競賽」，compete 最符合比賽情境
✗ fought(打鬥)、challenged(挑戰，需接受詞)、acted(表演)語意都不如 compete against 自然
空格 2However Moreover / However / Thus / So
語意前句講擔心科技威脅人類，後句說電腦只能執行人設定的程式，語意轉折用 However
✗ Moreover(此外，遞進)、Thus/So(因此，因果)都不符前後句的「轉折」關係
空格 3yet yet / still / only / just
搭配has not yet developed 是固定搭配（尚未發展），not...yet 表示「至今還沒」
時態搭配現在完成式 has not ... developed，yet 是常見副詞位置
✗ still 多放 not 之前(still has not)、only/just 語意是「只是/僅僅」，無法表達「尚未」
532.Bones5 格#080554
Bones also protect 1the organs in our bodies. The skull protects the brain and forms the shape of the face. The spinal cord, a pathway for messages between the brain and the body, is protected by the face. The spinal cord, a pathway for messages between the brain and the body, is protected by the backbone, or spinal column. The ribs form a cage that shelters the heart and lungs, and the pelvis helps protect the bladder, part of the intestines, and in women, the reproductive organs. Bones are helps protect the bladder, part of the intestines, and in women, the reproductive organs. Bones are made up of a framework of a protein called collagen, with a mineral called calcium phosphate that makes the framework hard and strong. Bones store calcium and release some into the bloodstream makes the framework hard and strong. Bones store calcium and release some into the bloodstream when it's needed by other parts of the body. The amounts of certain vitamins and minerals that you eat, especially vitamin D and calcium, directly affect how much calcium is stored in the bones. Joints eat, especially vitamin D and calcium, directly affect how much calcium is stored in the bones. Joints are where 2two bones meet. They make the skeleton flexible — without them, movement would be impossible. Joints allow our bodies to move in many ways. Some joints open and close like a hinge impossible. Joints allow our bodies to move in many ways. Some joints open and close like a hinge (such as knees and elbows), whereas others allow for more complicated movement — a shoulder or hip joint, for example, allows for backward, forward, sideways, and rotating movement. Joints are hip joint, for example, allows for backward, forward, sideways, and rotating movement. Joints are classified by their range of movement: Immovable, or fibrous, joints don't move. The dome of the skull, for example, is made of bony plates, which move slightly during birth and then fuse together as the for example, is made of bony plates, which move slightly during birth and then fuse together as the skull finishes growing. Between the edges of these plates are links, or joints, of fibrous tissue. Fibrous joints also hold the teeth in the jawbone. Partially movable, or cartilaginous, joints move a little. They joints also hold the teeth in the jawbone. Partially movable, or cartilaginous, joints move a little. They are linked by cartilage, as in the spine. Each of the vertebrae in the spine moves in relation to the one above and below it, and together these movements give the spine its flexibility. Freely movable, or above and below it, and together these movements give the spine its flexibility. Freely movable, or synovial (pronounced: sih-NO-vee-ul), joints move in many directions. The 3main joints of the body — such as those found at the hip, shoulders, elbows, knees, wrists, and ankles — are freely movable. They such as those found at the hip, shoulders, elbows, knees, wrists, and ankles — are freely movable. They are filled with synovial fluid, which acts as a lubricant to help the joints move easily. 4Three kinds 5of freely movable joints play a big part in voluntary movement: Hinge joints allow movement in one freely movable joints play a big part in voluntary movement: Hinge joints allow movement in one direction, as seen in the knees and elbows. Pivot joints allow a rotating or twisting motion, like that of the head moving from side to side. Ball-and-socket joints allow the greatest freedom of movement. the head moving from side to side. Ball-and-socket joints allow the greatest freedom of movement. The hips and shoulders have this type of joint, in which the round end of a long bone fits into the hollow of another bone. hollow of another bone. that which one two whole entire individual main All Two One Three Powered by TCPDF (www.tcpdf.org)
空格 1the a / that / our / the
語意messages between the brain and the ___ body，與前面 the brain 平行，指特定的「身體」，用定冠詞 the
詞性後接單數名詞 body，需冠詞；the 表特指人體
✗ a(泛指任一身體)不合特指語境；that 須有先行對照、our 改人稱不一致，皆不如 the 自然
空格 2two that / which / one / two
語意Joints are where ___ bones meet，關節是「兩塊」骨頭交會處，two bones
單複數後接複數 bones，且 meet(交會)需至少兩者，two 最合理
✗ that/which 為關係詞不能直接修飾 bones 當數量；one bone 無法 meet(單數無法交會)
空格 3main whole / entire / individual / main
搭配the main joints of the body 是搭配（人體主要關節）
語意後列舉 hip, shoulders, elbows... 為身體「主要」關節，用 main
✗ whole/entire(整個)修飾單一整體、individual(個別)語意相反，皆不符「主要關節」的概括
空格 4Three All / Two / One / Three
單複數後接複數 kinds，且下文列舉 Hinge、Pivot、Ball-and-socket 三種，故為 Three kinds
語意段落明確列出三類 freely movable joints，數字對應為 Three
✗ All kinds 太籠統不符後面確切三項列舉；Two/One 與實際列出的三種不符
空格 5of with / to / during / of
搭配range of movement 是固定搭配（活動範圍）
詞性名詞 range 後接介系詞 of 帶出所屬內容
✗ with/to/during 與 range 不搭配，無法表達「movement 的範圍」
